Activities such as outdoor play, creative arts and crafts, and family game nights contribute to a child’s growth and happiness.
The American Academy of Pediatrics acknowledges that parents often prioritize child safety over outdoor play opportunities when safe places to play are unavailable.

Outdoor play exposes toddlers to dynamic environments that promote physical health, emotional regulation, risk management, and connections with nature through activities like hiking, running, and gardening.
Outdoor play promotes physical health and improves motor skills in children, while shared family activities and social interactions improve communication skills.

Sugiyama et al. published a study in JAMA Pediatrics in 2023 indicating that outdoor play acts as a mitigating factor in the association between screen time for young children and neurodevelopmental outcomes.

Four non-communicable disease (NCD) dietary metrics—the Mediterranean Diet Score, Alternative Healthy Eating Index, Healthy Eating Index, and Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension—have convincing evidence of protective associations with specific NCD outcomes, including mortality, cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and total cancer.

Waning adherence to the Mediterranean Diet is linked to health risks, including chronic nutrition-related diseases, disability, and increased mortality, driven by increased consumption of lipids, processed foods, and simple carbohydrates, and a decrease in the consumption of complex carbohydrates.
Larger portion sizes in terms of energy, nutrient, and diversity in times of food abundance lead to unhealthy diets, diabesity, and diet-associated morbidity and mortality.
Bui et al. (2024) studied the Planetary Health Diet Index in relation to the risk of total and cause-specific mortality across three prospective cohorts.

Matsuyama S, Sawada N, Tomata Y et al. (2021) studied the association between adherence to the Japanese diet and all-cause and cause-specific mortality in the Japan Public Health Center-based prospective study, published in Eur J Nutr 60:1327–1336.
Olsen A, Egeberg R, Halkjær J et al. (2011) published a study in the Journal of Nutrition titled 'Healthy aspects of the Nordic diet are related to lower total mortality'.

Soltani et al. (2020) performed a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies regarding adherence to the Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension (DASH) diet and its relationship to all-cause and cause-specific mortality.

The research team developed 10 categories for health outcomes: cancer; cardiovascular diseases; mortality, number of deaths averted, or years of life saved (non-specific disease); type 2 diabetes; stroke; disability-adjusted life year (DALY) (non-specific disease); weight, overweight, or obesity; composite health indicators; quality-adjusted life year (QALY) or quality of life (QOL) related to non-specific diseases; or other.

Among the 95 health-related outcomes analyzed in the 42 reviewed papers, the most frequent categories were cancer (23.2%), heart-related diseases (21.1%), and mortality, number of deaths averted, or years of life saved (15.8%).

In the publications reviewed, researchers captured a total of 95 health and disease-related outcomes, with the most frequent categories being cancer (n=22, 23.2%), cardiovascular diseases (n=20, 21.1%), mortality/deaths averted/years of life saved (n=15, 15.8%), type 2 diabetes (n=12, 12.6%), and stroke (n=10, 10.5%).

The study 'Nut consumption and risk of cardiovascular disease, total cancer, all-cause and cause-specific mortality: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of prospective studies' by Aune D, Keum N, Giovannucci E, et al. was published in BMC Medicine in 2016 (14: 207).

The study 'Fruit and vegetable intake and the risk of cardiovascular disease, total cancer and all-cause mortality: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of prospective studies' by Aune D, Giovannucci E, Boffetta P, et al. was published in the International Journal of Epidemiology in 2016.

The study 'Body-mass index and all-cause mortality: individual-participant-data meta-analysis of 239 prospective studies in four continents' by Di Angelantonio E, Bhupathiraju S, et al. was published in The Lancet in 2016 (388: 776–86).

The study 'Whole grain consumption and risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all cause and cause specific mortality: systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of prospective studies' by Aune D, Keum N, Giovannucci E, et al. was published in the BMJ in 2016 (353: i2716).

The systematic analysis 'Global, regional, and national age-sex-specific mortality for 282 causes of death in 195 countries and territories, 1980–2017' was published in The Lancet in 2018 (392: 1736–88) as part of the Global Burden of Disease Study 2017.
The meta-analysis 'Fish consumption and all-cause mortality: a meta-analysis of cohort studies' was published in the European Journal of Clinical Nutrition in 2016 (70: 155–61).

The dose-response meta-analysis 'Fish consumption and risk of all-cause and cardiovascular mortality: a dose-response meta-analysis of prospective observational studies' was published in Public Health Nutrition in 2018 (21: 1297–306).
Greater adherence to a Western pattern diet increases the overall risk of mortality due to cancer.
Heidemann et al. (2008) conducted a prospective cohort study of women finding associations between dietary patterns and the risk of mortality from cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all causes.
Health outcome indicators primarily focused on morbidity or mortality related to chronic diseases, specifically coronary heart disease, stroke, type 2 diabetes, and certain cancers.

The DHD15-Index is justified because it reflects adherence to Dutch food-based dietary guidelines and is negatively correlated with mortality and cardiometabolic risk factors (Van Dooren et al., 2018b; Vellinga et al., 2019).

The authors of the editorial 'Dietary diversity indicators: cultural preferences and health outcomes' evaluated the relationship between the dietary diversity score (DDS) and mortality from all-cause, cardiovascular disease (CVD), and cancer in an adult Mediterranean population.
Previous epidemiological studies (citations 4–10) suggest that dietary diversity is closely associated with reduced mortality and lower incidence of diseases.
Increased consumption of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, legumes, and lean protein sources is associated with a reduced risk of metabolic diseases, type 2 diabetes, and mortality.
The Planetary Health Diet (PHD) aims to reduce the risk of non-communicable diseases and premature mortality by providing balanced food group recommendations.
The Joinpoint regression analysis model is a statistical method used in epidemiological research to evaluate the temporal trend of disease prevalence or mortality.
The incidence, prevalence, mortality, and Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) of dietary deficiencies differ significantly among regions.
Söderström et al. published 'Malnutrition is associated with increased mortality in older adults regardless of the cause of death' in the British Journal of Nutrition in 2017.
Malafarina et al. published 'Nutritional status and nutritional treatment are related to outcomes and mortality in older adults with hip fracture' in Nutrients in 2018.
Children of the Great Andamanese tribe in India experienced nutritional deficiency disorders and high mortality rates.
Epidemiological studies suggest that dietary diversity is closely associated with reduced mortality and lower incidence of diseases.
Changes in diet quality are associated with total and cause-specific mortality according to a 2017 study by Sotos-Prieto et al. published in the New England Journal of Medicine.
Jylhä (2009) proposed a unified conceptual model to explain what self-rated health is and why it predicts mortality, published in Social Science & Medicine.

E.L. Idler and Y. Benyamini published the review 'Self-rated health and mortality: a review of twenty-seven Community studies' in the Journal of Health and Social Behavior in 1997 (Volume 38, Issue 1, page 21).

G. Lorem, S. Cook, D.A. Leon, N. Emaus, and H. Schirmer published the cohort study 'Self-reported health as a predictor of mortality: a cohort study of its relation to other health measurements and observation time' in Scientific Reports in 2020 (Volume 10, Issue 1, article 4886).
Vulnerability and mortality are relevant factors for the evolution of valence, as indestructible systems immune to environmental dangers have little need for pain.
Chronic malnutrition is a major risk factor for global morbidity and mortality.
Vitamin A deficiency is a leading cause of morbidity and mortality among infants, children, and women in developing nations.
Vitamin A supplementation enhances immunity and reduces infection-related morbidity and mortality associated with vitamin A deficiency.
Obesity is an independent risk factor for increased morbidity and mortality following infection with the 2009 influenza A (H1N1) virus.

Routine prophylactic supplementation with iron and folic acid was evaluated for its effects on hospital admission and mortality in preschool children in a high malaria transmission setting in a 2006 randomized, placebo-controlled trial published in The Lancet.
Untreated chronic inflammation generally carries a poor prognosis, with morbidity and mortality dependent on the specific causative mechanistic process.

Inflammation is associated with the morbidity and mortality of cardiovascular disease, chronic kidney disease, diabetes mellitus, non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, cancer, autoimmune diseases, neurodegenerative disorders, and behavioral health disorders.

The Emerging Risk Factors Collaboration published a meta-analysis in The Lancet in 2010 finding that C-reactive protein concentration is associated with the risk of coronary heart disease, stroke, and mortality.

Elevated systemic immune inflammation levels increase the risk of total and cause-specific mortality among patients with chronic kidney disease, according to a 2023 multi-center longitudinal study published in Inflammation Research.
Low-grade inflammation serves as a risk factor for cardiovascular events and all-cause mortality in patients with type 2 diabetes, as reported in a 2021 study in Cardiovascular Diabetology.
The systemic immune inflammatory index is associated with all-cause and cause-specific mortality among individuals with type 2 diabetes, according to a 2023 study in BMC Cardiovascular Disorders.
The systemic inflammation response index is a predictor of all-cause mortality in patients with antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody-associated vasculitis, as reported by Lee et al. in 2021.
Ultraprocessed food consumption is associated with an increased risk of mortality among middle-aged adults in France, according to a 2019 study by Schnabel et al. published in JAMA Internal Medicine.

Studies of patients at sleep clinics tend to show an association between sleep apnea and mortality, but several population-based studies have failed to find this association, according to Young et al. (2002a,b), Lavie et al. (2005), He et al. (1988), Ancoli-Israel et al. (1996), Lindberg et al. (1998), and Kripke et al. (2002).
Three large, population-based, prospective studies involving cohorts ranging from 83,000 to 1.1 million people found that sleep loss is associated with increased age-specific mortality.
Sleeping 5 hours or less per night is associated with an increased mortality risk from all causes by approximately 15 percent.
Epidemiological studies indicate that mortality related to sleep loss is largely attributed to acute heart attacks.
Bliwise et al. investigated the relationship between sleep apnea and mortality in an aged cohort.
A 1988 study of 385 male patients by He et al. analyzed the relationship between mortality and the apnea index in patients with obstructive sleep apnea.
A subgroup of patients below age 60 with both snoring and excessive daytime sleepiness experienced twice the risk of mortality, according to Lindberg et al. (1998).
Kripke DF, Garfinkel L, Wingard DL, Klauber MR, and Marler MR reported in 2002 that mortality is associated with sleep duration and insomnia.
Lavie P, Lavie L, and Herer P reported in 2005 that all-cause mortality rates in males with sleep apnoea syndrome decline with age.
Daytime sleepiness is a predictor of mortality and cardiovascular disease in older adults.
Three large, population-based, prospective studies (Kripke et al., 2002; Tamakoshi et al., 2004; Patel et al., 2004) found that sleep loss is associated with increased age-specific mortality.

An observational follow-up study found that untreated sleep apnea patients (n=61) were more likely to die from cardiovascular disease compared to those treated with CPAP therapy for at least 5 years (n=107), with mortality rates of 14.8 percent versus 1.9 percent.
S. Ancoli-Israel and colleagues examined the relationship between morbidity, mortality, and sleep-disordered breathing in community-dwelling elderly in a 1996 study published in Sleep.
Kripke et al. (2002) found an association between sleep duration, insomnia, and mortality rates in a study published in Archives in General Psychiatry.
Lindberg E et al. found increased mortality among sleepy snorers in a prospective population-based study published in Thorax in 1998.
Newman et al. (2000) determined that daytime sleepiness predicts mortality and cardiovascular disease in older adults.

In the GAZEL cohort study, which assessed sleep disturbances using the 5-item sleep dimension from the Nottingham Health Profile, sleep disturbance was associated with a higher all-cause risk of mortality in men (P = 0.005), but not in women (P = 0.33).

Men who reported sleep disruption on the Nottingham Health Profile had a higher all-cause mortality risk compared with those who did not report sleep disruption, with a hazard ratio of 1.69 and a 95% confidence interval of 1.25–2.31.
The high correlation between sleep disturbances, depression, and suicidal ideation may play a role in identifying an increased risk of mortality.
Sleep disorders are linked to mortality through an increase in cardiovascular deaths, which are also related to sleep disruption.
Researchers suggest that additional studies in larger cohorts, while controlling for confounding factors, are needed to further understand the relationship between sleep disorders and mortality.

While hypertension and diabetes may not explain death in younger individuals with sleep disruption, the association of sleep disruption with these conditions is a risk factor for mortality in later life.
The GAZEL cohort study found an association between sleep disturbances and cause-specific mortality in a 2011 analysis published in the American Journal of Epidemiology.
Naps lasting longer than 1.5 hours are associated with worse outcomes for cardiovascular health, diabetes, cognitive function, and increased mortality in older adults.
The association between sleep loss and diabetes or impaired glucose tolerance may mediate the relationship between sleep loss and cardiovascular morbidity and mortality.
Daytime sleepiness predicts mortality and cardiovascular disease in older adults, according to a 2000 study by Newman et al. published in the Journal of the American Geriatric Society.
The three prospective studies on sleep loss and mortality involved cohorts ranging from 83,000 to 1.1 million people.
The three prospective studies on sleep loss and mortality followed respondents for periods ranging from 6 to 14 years after surveying them about their sleep duration.

The three prospective studies on sleep loss and mortality compared mortality rates of short or long sleepers against a reference group of individuals who slept 7 hours, adjusting for health and demographic factors.
The largest American study on sleep loss and mortality illustrates a U-shaped curve, showing that both progressively shorter and longer sleep durations are associated with greater mortality.
Bliwise DL, Bliwise NG, Partinen M, Pursley AM, and Dement WC studied sleep apnea and mortality in an aged cohort, published in the American Journal of Public Health.
The consequences of sleep conditions negatively impact mortality, morbidity, performance, accidents and injuries, functioning and quality of life, family well-being, and health care utilization.
Several population-based studies failed to find an association between sleep apnea and mortality, according to Ancoli-Israel et al. (1996), Lindberg et al. (1998), and Kripke et al. (2002).
Berkman and Syme (1979) conducted a nine-year follow-up study of Alameda County residents, finding that social networks are linked to host resistance and mortality.
Social support and social integration are predictive of mortality for a number of conditions, including cardiovascular disease (CVD).
Kornej et al. (2022) analyzed the association between social network index, atrial fibrillation, and mortality in the Framingham Heart Study.

Pantell M, Rehkopf D, Jutte D, Syme SL, Balmes J, and Adler N identified social isolation as a predictor of mortality comparable to traditional clinical risk factors in a 2013 study published in the American Journal of Public Health.
Combined proteomic and metabolomic profiling in Long COVID patients facilitates the discovery of candidate biomarkers and enables the prediction of disease severity and mortality.
Hajat, Kaufman, Rose, Siddiqi, and Thomas (2011) examined the long-term effects of wealth on mortality and self-rated health status in a study published in the American Journal of Epidemiology.

The book 'Deaths of Despair and the Future of Capitalism' by Anne Case and Angus Deaton, published by Princeton University Press in 2020, examines the relationship between economic conditions and mortality.
The traditional global balance of power, historically dominated by the United States, is being reshaped by an evolving multipolar world structure.

The European Union's ability to develop a single strategic response to the multipolar world is hampered by the economic divide between northern and southern member states, the east-west divide regarding Russia and China, and divergent perceptions of threats.

Vladimir Putin's ultimate geopolitical goal is to establish a 'multipolar' world order by joining with China, the BRICS nations, and other countries to diminish the United States' ability to shape international rules.
The global distribution of power is shifting toward a multipolar world, marking the end of Western hegemony that has persisted for the last two centuries.
The multipolar world of 2030–2045 will not necessarily consist of regional blocs that speak with a single voice.

The Kurdish question and the Palestinian Crises are identified as two primary, prolonged issues that could escalate tensions between global powers and contribute to the emergence of a new multipolar global order.

Iran's 'Look East' policy, which prioritizes relations with China and Russia, is driven by economic imperatives resulting from Western sanctions, dual security considerations involving power projection and defensive deterrence, and an aspiration to position Iran within an anticipated multipolar global order.
Iran envisions a multipolar world order and aspires to play a role in achieving this structure within the Gulf region.
Tehran perceives a synergy between the Russian vision for a new global order and its own, viewing this alignment as the most effective path to establishing a multipolar world system.
Iran's desire for a multipolar world system motivates its efforts to increase its relevance and influence in regional strategic calculations.
George Armelagos believes that examining Paleolithic nutrition is useful for understanding the origins of current health problems rather than simply reverting to ancient eating habits.
The modern diet is an integral part of life in both developed and developing nations and is associated with a massive increase in obesity and related health problems.
Sleep is influenced by a mix of biological and environmental factors, often in line with major life events, health issues, lifestyle choices, and sociodemographic factors.
Chronic sleep deprivation can cause or contribute to a variety of health issues.

The United States could pursue collaboration with China on health and climate issues, such as the ecology of infectious diseases, as well as green energy, tariff-for-market-access swaps, and a return to multilateralism, according to Bouey.
Individuals facing unexpected health issues often prioritize medical expenses over savings, which leads to immediate financial adjustments.
Sleep problems after cancer treatment can be caused by other health problems, pain, medicines, caffeine, shift work, and coping methods.

The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change and the Fourth National Climate Assessment reports indicate that emissions backsliding is unacceptable and that climate change impacts are currently occurring.
The fourth National Climate Assessment (NCA), released in 2017, involved the efforts of thirteen federal agencies led by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
The fourth National Climate Assessment involved approximately 1,000 people, including 300 leading scientists, with roughly half of the participants coming from outside the government.
The U.S. Global Change Research Program (USGCRP) published Chapter 3, 'Detection and Attribution of Climate Change', as part of the Fourth National Climate Assessment, Volume I.
The U.S. Global Change Research Program published the 'Climate Science Special Report' as Volume I of the Fourth National Climate Assessment (NCA4) on 23 November 2018.
The Fourth National Climate Assessment (NCA) was released in 2017.

Knowledge silos occur when different teams or departments use separate systems for storing content, which leads to missed opportunities for cross-functional collaboration, goal misalignment, wasted time searching for information, and duplication of work.

Organizations can address knowledge silos and information hoarding by implementing a company-wide knowledge management platform to replace separate team-specific platforms, thereby increasing the visibility of completed work.

Information hoarding and knowledge silos in the workplace often stem from a lack of time for documentation, a lack of structured guidelines for knowledge sharing, or the use of disparate storage systems by different teams or departments.

Information hoarding and knowledge silos create barriers to effective communication by causing missed opportunities for cross-functional collaboration, goal misalignment, wasted time spent searching for information, and duplication of work across an organization.

Organizations can address information hoarding and knowledge silos by implementing a company-wide knowledge management platform to replace separate, team-specific storage systems, thereby increasing the visibility of completed work and improving the efficiency of knowledge sharing.

Prior to the advent of Large Language Models, Knowledge Graph construction stages were implemented through rule-based, statistical, and symbolic approaches.

Machine learning approaches to ontology learning include statistic-based methods, such as co-occurrence analysis and clustering, and logic-based approaches, such as inductive logic programming or logical inference.

To maximize the potential of within-subject longitudinal sleep studies, researchers should implement regular follow-ups, provide appropriate participant compensation, utilize flexible data collection methods such as remote completion, and employ statistical methods capable of handling missing data.

aliases: electrification of industrial and domestic processes

facts.content: Electrification of industrial and domestic processes reduces fossil fuel consumption for powering industrial boilers, furnaces, and process equipment, which requires research and development into efficient heating systems and the reduction of unit power costs.

Electrification of industries and homes using renewable energy sources is a strategy to reduce global greenhouse gas emissions, which currently originate from sectors including extraction/refining, power generation, transport, agriculture, industry, and residential use.
China appears to be utilizing the South China Sea as a protected bastion for nuclear-armed submarines to safeguard its second-strike capability against the United States.
China fears that US missile defense initiatives and conventional force expansion threaten its second-strike capability and nuclear deterrent.
Beijing fears that United States missile defense initiatives and the expansion of conventional US forces threaten China's second-strike capability and its nuclear deterrent.
China fears that US missile defense initiatives and the expansion of conventional US forces threaten its second-strike capability and its nuclear deterrent against the United States.

Nuclear-armed great powers interpret routine military exercises as potential cover for preemptive strikes executed at machine speeds, and they view AI-enabled intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) improvements as direct threats to their second-strike capabilities.
Clark Spencer Larsen of Ohio State University asserts that the diets of early farmers were far less nutritionally diverse than the diets of hunter-gatherers.
Clark Spencer Larsen states that early farmers suffered from cavities and periodontal disease due to a diet of domesticated grains, conditions rarely found in hunter-gatherers.
Clark Spencer Larsen stated, “you don’t have to be disease free to have children.”

US-China relations under Donald Trump's second term have entered a fragile stalemate characterized by a shift toward strategic and psychological disengagement rather than complete economic decoupling.

US-China relations under the second term of President Donald Trump have entered a fragile stalemate characterized by strategic and psychological disengagement, where both nations are reducing reliance on each other's long-term strategies.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps-Quds Force (IRGC-QF) organized between 10,000 and 15,000 Afghan militants into the Fatemiyoun Brigade and deployed them to Syria to fight alongside pro-Assad forces.
Fatemiyoun Brigade fighters were deployed in battles in Aleppo, Daraa, Damascus, Hama, Homs, Latakia, Palmyra, and Dayr az Zawr.

The Trump administration (2017–2021) sanctioned seven groups (Ansar Allah, Asaib Ahl al Haq, Harakat Hezbollah al Nujaba, Zaynabiyoun Brigade, Fatemiyoun Division, Al Ashtar Brigades, Saraya al Mukhtar) and 32 leaders.
Tobias Schneider authored a report titled 'The Fatemiyoun Division' for The Middle East Institute in October 2018, detailing the Fatemiyoun Division.
The Fatemiyoun Division is an Afghan militia founded in the 1980s, which went dormant in the 1990s and was revived by Iran’s Revolutionary Guards in 2012.
The Fatemiyoun Division is primarily composed of Hazara refugees from Afghanistan living in Iran.
Since 2014, Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have deployed the Fatemiyoun Division to fight for the Assad regime in Syria.
In 2017, approximately 50,000 Afghans were deployed in Syria as part of the Fatemiyoun Division.
The U.S. Treasury Department sanctioned the Fatemiyoun Division in 2019 for supporting the Qods Force and engaging in human rights abuses in Iran.

The nucleosome, which is the basic unit of chromatin, consists of pairs of core histones H2A, H2B, H3, and H4 and a DNA strand of 146 base pairs.
ATP-dependent chromatin remodelers, known as sucrose nonfermenting 2 (SNF2) or switch/SNF (SWI/SNF)-related enzymes, utilize energy released during ATP hydrolysis to rebuild chromatin.
Transcription can be modulated by alternate splicing, different promoter or enhancer usage, non-coding RNAs, and epigenetic changes that impact the structure and function of chromatin.
Menoni et al. (2017) identified nucleosome remodeling and DNA transcription as two key mechanisms associated with chromatin during base excision repair.
Chromatin and metabolism are interconnected, as reviewed by Suganuma and Workman in their 2018 Annual Review of Biochemistry paper.
PARP inhibitors induce death in homologous recombination-deficient (HRD) tumor cells by physically trapping the PARP1 enzyme at DNA break sites on chromatin.

Advantage Reweighting is a method proposed by Yang et al. (2025b) to balance token-level contributions, which is essential for stable optimization in complex reasoning tasks.

The awareness of pain serves as an adaptive function of consciousness by enabling organisms to avoid perilous situations in the future.
To The Stars Inc. was founded in October 2017 by Tom DeLonge, Harold E. Puthoff, and Jim Semivan, with personnel including Christopher Mellon and Luis Elizondo.
In December 2017, To The Stars Inc. provided the first of what became known as the "Pentagon UFO videos" to the press.

A 1994 report by the American Psychiatry Association found that the incidence of schizophrenia occurs at roughly the same rate in Western and non-Western cultures, as well as in industrialized and pastoral societies, suggesting it is not a disease of civilization or an arbitrary social invention.

Sleep disturbances, including insomnia, excessive daytime sleepiness (hypersomnia), and parasomnia, are common features of psychiatric disorders and are listed as diagnostic criteria in the DSM-IV (APA, 1994).
The American Psychiatric Association published the 4th edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-IV).

The American Psychiatric Association states that cognitive behavioral therapy “can help a person learn a different way of thinking, reacting and behaving”, which can potentially alleviate both the mental and physical symptoms of anxiety.
The Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition, Text Revision (DSM-IV-TR) is an official manual of mental health problems developed by the American Psychiatric Association.
The American Psychiatric Association published the 'DSM-5 handbook on the cultural formulation interview' in 2016.

The PROMIS Emotional Distress–Anxiety–Short Form for adults and the Severity Measure for Generalized Anxiety Disorder–Adult are tools intended to aid clinical evaluation of Generalized Anxiety Disorder and monitor treatment effectiveness, available from the American Psychiatric Association.
The American Psychiatric Association (2022) recommends that counselors perform safety risk assessments and monitoring for clients with Generalized Anxiety Disorder.

Agoraphobia is defined by the American Psychiatric Association in the DSM-IV-TR as anxiety about being in situations or places where escape might be difficult, embarrassing, or where help might be unavailable during an unexpected panic attack or panic-like symptoms.

The American Psychiatric Association defines the diagnostic criteria for social anxiety disorder in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition, Text Revision (DSM-IV-TR) as including the fear that an individual will act in a way or show anxiety symptoms that will be humiliating or embarrassing.
The American Psychiatric Association published the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition, Text Revision (DSM-IV-TR) in Washington, DC, in 2000.

The American Psychiatric Association states that materials regarding DSM-IV-TR criteria are for educational purposes only and are not a substitute for a diagnosis made by a qualified mental health professional.

The American Psychiatric Association officially recognized Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) as a diagnosis in 1980 with the publication of the third edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-III).
The American Psychiatric Association published 'Mental Health Disparities: Diverse Populations' in 2017, which addresses mental health disparities among diverse populations.

Benedek DM, Friedman MJ, Zatzick D, and Ursano RJ authored the 'Guideline watch: practice guideline for the treatment of patients with acute stress disorder and posttraumatic stress disorder', published by the American Psychiatric Association in 2009.
The American Psychiatric Association published a practice guideline for the treatment of patients with obsessive-compulsive disorder in 2013, authored by Koran LM and Simpson HB.
The recognition of PTSD by the American Psychiatric Association followed the return of thousands of American combat veterans from the Vietnam War who displayed symptoms of the disorder.

Disinhibited attachment disorder, listed in ICD-10 (World Health Organization, 2010) and relabelled as 'disinhibited social engagement disorder' (DSED) in DSM-5 (American Psychiatric Association, 2013), is characterized by a marked and pervasive tendency to not show appropriate cautiousness with unfamiliar adults and a failure to be sensitive to social boundaries.

The Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, fifth edition (DSM-5), published by the American Psychiatric Association in 2013, uses the term 'disinhibited social engagement disorder' to describe what was previously referred to as disinhibited attachment disorder.

The American Psychiatric Association's Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders defines childhood separation anxiety disorder in children younger than 18 years as exhibiting three of the following: excessive distress about actual or anticipated separation from home or parents; persistent concern about losing parents or harm coming to them; persistent fear of being lost or kidnapped; persistent reluctance to go to school; persistent reluctance to be alone; persistent reluctance to sleep away from home or without a parent; repeated nightmares about separation; or repeated complaints of physical symptoms like headaches when anticipating separation.
The American Psychiatric Association published the fourth edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-IV) in 1994.

The American Psychiatric Association published a task force report titled 'The use of psychiatric diagnoses in the legal process' in the Bulletin of the American Academy of Psychiatry and the Law in 1992.
The American Psychiatric Association Board of Trustees approved a 'Statement on memories of sexual abuse' on December 12, 1993.

The American Medical Association and the American Psychiatric Association have issued statements cautioning against assuming that recovered repressed memories are inherently accurate and reliable, noting that external corroboration is necessary to distinguish true memories from imagined events.

In December 1993, the Board of Trustees of the American Psychiatric Association responded to debates regarding repressed memories, fearing that the debates may have obscured the recognition of scientific evidence regarding memory research and proper therapeutic techniques.

The American Psychiatric Association 1992 Task Force views the inference that an individual will develop a particular set of symptoms in response to repeated traumatic events as a 'misuse of psychiatric expertise.'
The American Psychiatric Association has condemned past life regression therapy, labeling it as 'pure quackery.'
The American Psychiatric Association Board of Trustees approved a 'Statement on memories of sexual abuse' on December 12, 1993.

The American Psychiatric Association's Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-III) lists three factors, and the DSM-IV lists four additional factors, to be considered as criteria for Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) that overlap with several other diagnoses.

The American Psychiatric Association published the report 'Chapter 4. Personality Disorders and Relational Disorders: A Research Agenda for Addressing Crucial Gaps in DSM' in 2002 as part of 'A research agenda for DSM-V'.
A survey from the American Psychiatric Association found that 59% of adults reported feeling anxious about their personal finances.

Staphylococcus aureus infects hosts by breaching skin or mucous membranes, leading to severe conditions such as bloodstream infections, pneumonia, systemic infections, joint infections, and osteomyelitis.
In severe cases, localized wound inflammation may progress to systemic infections, which require prompt and aggressive treatment.
Systemic symptoms of wound inflammation include fever, which may occur in cases of severe or systemic infection, and general malaise, which is a feeling of being unwell or fatigued.

Management of systemic infection involves addressing signs such as fever or sepsis with medical interventions, which may include surgical intervention to remove infected tissue, drain abscesses, or repair damaged structures.

Infection in a wound can lead to increased risk of chronic wounds, delayed wound closure, increased pain and discomfort, increased risk of systemic infection, increased healthcare costs, increased scarring, and reduced patient quality of life.
The technical potential for rooftop solar photovoltaic, wave energy, and tidal energy is estimated to be above 1 PWh/year.
Marine energy resources primarily consist of tidal power, wave power, and ocean thermal energy.

Increasing generation from wave and tidal power requires policy incentives and funding, as the primary challenge for marine power is that supply is not continuous, though it is relatively predictable.

Weak reductionism holds that consciousness is a simple or basic phenomenon that cannot be informatively broken down into simpler nonconscious elements.

Nonreductive and dualistic views agree with weak reductionism that phenomenal properties underwrite first-person access, but they reject the idea that these properties are physical.
Weak reductionism relies on a claim of parsimony and a commitment to physicalism, despite the lack of evidence that phenomenal properties are physical.
Weak reductionists argue that consciousness can be identified with physical properties if the most parsimonious and productive theory supports such an identity.

To overcome negative body language, individuals should ensure their nonverbal communication aligns with their verbal communication, seek feedback from trusted colleagues or mentors regarding their nonverbal habits, and practice positive body language such as maintaining eye contact, using open gestures, and nodding.

Richard Newman, the founder of Body Talk, successfully used nonverbal communication and body language to connect with Tibetan monks while teaching them English, despite not sharing a common spoken language.

Factors that lead to communication barriers include differences in culture and language, as well as semantic issues stemming from misperceptions in body language, facial expressions, or nonverbal communication.
Communication breakdowns in the workplace can occur across verbal, written, and nonverbal communication channels.

Nonverbal communication, such as hand gestures, body language, and facial expressions, can hold different meanings across cultures, requiring employees to be sensitive to their environment to avoid misunderstandings.

Organizations can address differences in communication styles by following these steps: (1) incorporate communication style assessments into the onboarding process to identify employee preferences, (2) conduct leadership training on all facets of dialogue, including nonverbal and paraverbal communication, (3) store information about communication approaches in the company's knowledge management system, and (4) embed active listening and the practice of asking clarifying questions into the company culture.

Factors that lead to common communication barriers include cultural differences, language differences, and semantic issues stemming from misperceptions in body language, facial expressions, or nonverbal communication.

The medium chosen for written communication, such as email, formal letter, tweet, or online ad, constitutes a form of nonverbal communication that influences the recipient's perception of the message and the sender's company.

Nonverbal communication, including hand gestures, body language, and facial expressions, can hold different meanings across cultures, requiring employees to be sensitive to their environment before using them.

To address differences in communication styles, organizations should: (1) incorporate communication style assessments into the onboarding process to identify employee preferences, (2) conduct leadership training on all facets of dialogue including nonverbal and paraverbal communication, (3) store information about communication approaches in the company's knowledge management system, and (4) embed active listening and the practice of asking clarifying questions into the company culture.

Factors that lead to common communication barriers include cultural and language differences, as well as semantic issues stemming from misperceptions in body language, facial expressions, or nonverbal communication.
Effective nonverbal communication is essential for both face-to-face interactions and virtual meetings.
93% of communication is nonverbal, including body language, tone, and facial expressions.

Graph-structured data captures relationships between entities and provides structural information, which enables Large Language Models (LLMs) to interpret external knowledge more effectively.

Non-Euclidean space is proposed as a more efficient representation space than Euclidean space for handling non-linear problems in neuro-symbolic AI, such as complex relationships, graph-structured data, and timing dependencies.
Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) excel in tasks such as node classification, link prediction, and the extraction of hidden patterns from graph-structured data.
Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) extend neural architectures to graph-structured data, enabling advanced reasoning over interconnected entities.
Zhou et al. (2022) demonstrated that 'least-to-most prompting' enables complex reasoning in large language models.
GraphRAG improves information retrieval for complex reasoning tasks by leveraging Knowledge Graphs.

Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) enhances Large Language Model performance on tasks requiring external knowledge by leveraging Knowledge Graphs to improve information retrieval for complex reasoning tasks.
Experimental results indicate that KG-IRAG improves accuracy in complex reasoning tasks by integrating external knowledge with iterative, logic-based retrieval.
Large Language Models (LLMs) have limited capacity for complex reasoning on large amounts of data input without substantial model fine-tuning.
Datadog's prompt optimization approach is based on the principle that LLMs are more effective at guided summarization than complex reasoning.
Wang et al. (2024c) and Chi et al. (2023) improve complex reasoning in LLMs by utilizing symbolic and adaptive memory structures.
CAQA (Hu et al., 2024a) is a Knowledge-Based Question Answering (KBQA) dataset that evaluates complex reasoning and attribution.

Prior to the enactment of the Classified Information Procedures Act (CIPA) in 1980, defendants with intelligence ties could avoid prosecution by threatening to expose government secrets, a tactic known as 'graymail'.

The Classified Information Procedures Act (CIPA), enacted in 1980, provides a mechanism for prosecutors to remove specific pieces of evidence to avoid exposing sensitive intelligence to defense attorneys, which requires filing motions that would be noted on the public court docket.
A search of the federal court dockets for the cases against Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell found no mention of Classified Information Procedures Act (CIPA) litigation.
The Classified Information Procedures Act was passed by Congress in 1980 to protect classified information used in criminal trials.

Hickey stated regarding the Classified Information Procedures Act (CIPA): 'The way things normally happen is that there would be some tell in the docket that you're dealing with these issues, and the court knows about it through CIPA.'
The Stimulus Organism Response (SOR) model is used by Hashmi, Attiq, and Rasheed (2019) to analyze factors affecting online impulsive buying behavior.
The study on advertising value and online impulsive buying behavior collected data from 1422 online consumers and analyzed the data using structural equation modeling.
Customer anxiety does not have a moderating impact on the urge to buy online impulsively and online impulsive buying behavior.
The S-O-R (Stimulus-Organism-Response) framework provides a theoretical foundation for understanding the dynamics between advertising values, internal processes, and online impulsive buying behavior.
Online impulsive buying behavior is defined as the spontaneous and unplanned act of purchasing products online driven by impulsive urges or desires.

Advertisement informativeness in the context of online impulsive buying behavior refers to the extent to which advertisements provide relevant, timely, accurate, and valuable information about products or services.

In the context of online impulsive buying behavior, advertising entertainment refers to advertisements that create a pleasant and enjoyable experience, evoke positive emotions, provide relaxation, and stimulate the mind.

The 'urge to buy impulsively' (UI) in the context of online impulsive buying behavior is defined as the spontaneous desire to make unplanned purchases while browsing advertisements or encountering products online.

The survey instrument for the study 'Modeling the significance of advertising values on online impulse' evaluated hypotheses regarding advertising value, online impulsive buying behavior (OIB), and the moderating role of consumer anxiety (CA).

The study 'Modeling the significance of advertising values on online impulse' utilized independent constructs from earlier investigations, including informativeness, credibility, creativity, entertainment, interaction, integration, user interface (UI), consumer anxiety (CA), and online impulsive buying behavior (OIB).
The study's focus on online impulsive buying behavior may limit the generalizability of the findings to offline retail or other product categories.

Lina Y, Hou D, and Ali S (2022) published 'Impact of online convenience on generation Z online impulsive buying behavior: The moderating role of social media celebrity' in Frontiers in Psychology, volume 13, article 951249.

I.I. Kirsch and S.J. Lynn's 1996 article 'Alleged Alien Abductions: False Memories, Hypnosis and Fantasy Proneness', published in Psychological Inquiry, explores the psychological mechanisms of false memories, hypnosis, and fantasy proneness in the context of alleged alien abductions.
Fantasy proneness is a personality trait where individuals deeply engage in fantasies, blurring the line between reality and imagination.
Psychological traits such as dissociativity and fantasy proneness are not unique to those who report alien abductions, and not all individuals with these traits report such experiences.
Fantasy proneness, which involves confusing imaginary and real events, may incline individuals to view theories of alien consciousness as profound and desirable.

Caroline C. McLeod, Barbara Corbisier, and John E. Mack published 'A More Parsimonious Explanation for UFO Abduction' in the 1996 issue of 'Psychological Inquiry,' which critiques the arguments made by Leonard S. Newman and Roy F. Baumeister regarding the relationship between fantasy proneness, masochistic fantasy, and hypnotic elaboration.

Critics argue that memories retrieved via hypnotic regression in abduction cases may be pseudo-memories caused by pressure to recall, false-memory syndrome caused by the hypnotist's suggestions, or fantasy proneness where the subject hallucinates experiences that feel subjectively real.
Robert E. Bartholomew, Keith Basterfield, and G. S. Howard published 'UFO Abductees and Contactees: Psychopathology or Fantasy Proneness?' in Professional Psychology: Research and Practice in 1991.

In their article 'Not Just Another False Memory: Further Thoughts on the UFO Abduction Phenomenon,' Leonard S. Newman and Roy F. Baumeister discussed the role of hypnosis in the creation of abduction memories and the characteristics of a fantasy-prone person.

The 2001 study on UFO experiences found that reporting such experiences is related to heightened levels of fantasy proneness and paranormal belief, with the relationship between fantasy proneness and UFO experiences being mediated by paranormal belief.
Arctic resident fish species typically perform limited migrations within Arctic waters, moving between spawning, feeding, and overwintering areas.

Arctic cod (Boreogadus saida) perform seasonal movements inshore to shallow waters during summer for juvenile development, adult feeding, and pre-spawning schooling, while moving to deeper areas for spawning and overwintering below sea ice.

Tracking data indicates that while some seabird species maintain consistent overwintering areas across years (Orben et al., 2015b), other studies show individuals exhibit flexibility and variability in their overwintering locations (Guilford et al., 2011).

The ivory gull faces a high risk of population decline and potential extinction before the end of the century due to changes in the ice habitat used for migration and overwintering, combined with the impact of receding summer sea ice near breeding colonies.

Frederiksen, M., Descamps, S., Erikstad, K. E., Gaston, A. J., Gilchrist, H. G., Grémillet, D., et al. (2016) published 'Migration and wintering of a declining seabird, the thick-billed murre Uria lomvia, on an ocean basin scale: conservation implications' in Biological Conservation, volume 200, pages 26–35, investigating the migration and wintering patterns of the thick-billed murre.
Thick-billed murres in breeding pairs migrate and overwinter in locations far apart from each other but within similar photic environments, according to a 2024 study by Huffeldt et al.
Taygetans utilize a royal-matriarchal-holographic political structure.
Taygetans are classified as a Nordic race of Lyrian descent.
Taygetans physically resemble Scandinavian humans and inhabit the four planets of the Taygeta Tau-19 solar system located in the M45 Pleiades star cluster.

Taygetans fulfill different roles on their ship and are specialized in multiple fields.
The Taygetans physically resemble Scandinavian humans.
The Taygetans inhabit the four planets of the Taygeta Tau-19 sun solar system located in the M45 Pleiades star cluster.
