Taygetans are described as a culture utilizing a royal-matriarchal-holographic political structure and are classified as a Nordic race of Lyrian descent.

Liability-relative asset allocation is an investment framework that accounts for the liabilities a portfolio is intended to fund, and it includes a specific extension of mean–variance optimization known as surplus optimization.
Surplus optimization is an economic balance sheet approach that extends to the liability side of the balance sheet to identify the most efficient asset class mixes when liabilities are present.
Approaches to liability-relative asset allocation include surplus optimization, a hedging/return-seeking portfolios approach, and an integrated asset–liability approach.
Surplus optimization is an approach to liability-relative asset allocation that involves applying Mean-Variance Optimization (MVO) to surplus returns.

Philosopher Erich Fromm defined 'rational authority' as the authority granted to individuals who possess special areas of competence, such as teachers, scientists, therapists, surgeons, plumbers, and pilots.

Social psychologist Erich Fromm diagnosed capitalist society as fostering a “having mode” of existence, which is obsessed with ownership and consumption, rather than a “being mode” of expressing one’s inner self creatively.
Erich Fromm diagnosed capitalist society as fostering a “having mode” of existence, which is obsessed with ownership and consumption, rather than a “being mode” of creative self-expression.
The study 'Sleep disturbance and risk behaviors among inner-city African-American adolescents' by Umlauf, Bolland, and Lian was published in the Journal of Urban Health in 2011.

The prevalence of alcohol use is higher for boys than for girls, higher for White and Hispanic adolescents than for African-American adolescents, and higher for those in the North and north-central United States than for those living in the South and West.

Alcohol use and problem drinking in late adolescence vary by socioeconomic characteristics, with higher prevalence for boys than girls, higher for White and Hispanic adolescents than African-American adolescents, and higher for those in the North and north-central United States than those in the South and West.

aliases: Dietary Diversity Score at the household level

facts.content: The Dietary Diversity Score at the household level (HDDS) is used as a proxy for the socioeconomic level of a household, reflecting the economic ability of a household to consume a variety of foods.
The abbreviation HDDS stands for Dietary Diversity Score at the household level.
Significant measures of dietary diversity include Household Dietary Diversity Score (HDDS) and Individual Dietary Diversity Score (IDDS).

A study using data from a cluster-randomized trial in Nicaragua (2000-2002) investigated whether a conditional cash-transfer program's impact on child nutritional outcomes was mediated by health check-ups and household dietary diversity.

The controlled direct effect (CDE) of the conditional cash-transfer program on child height-for-age z scores was 0.20 standard deviations (95% CI: -0.17, 0.57), suggesting that nearly one-half of the program's impact on child nutrition would be eliminated if health check-ups and household dietary diversity were held constant.
Digitalis has effects on the autonomic nervous system, as noted by Watanabe in the Journal of the American College of Cardiology (1985).

Herbal medicines are categorized into three safety groups: the first includes highly toxic herbs like Atropa belladonna, Aconitum, Arnica, and Digitalis that require expert supervision; the second includes herbs with strong physiological effects like Lobelia and Euonymus that are generally safe when used correctly; and the third includes herbs like Comfrey, Yohimbe, and Mistletoe that have known toxicities, particularly hepatotoxic effects, and are not recommended for internal use by non-professionals.
An English physician learned about the 'secret family recipe' for digitalis from a wise woman herbalist in 1775.
Giannis (2025) authored a chapter titled 'Strengthening the sick heart (foxglove, Digitalis)' in the book 'Natural compounds enabled therapies'.
The speaker in the 'Meta Mystics' podcast asserts that past life regression allows individuals to observe their own death in a past life and experience the subsequent events.

The speaker in the 'Meta Mystics' podcast claims that while individuals may fear death prior to hypnosis, those who undergo the process report that the transition from death to the afterlife or the next incarnation feels natural.
The research paper 'What formal languages can transformers express? a survey' provides a survey of the formal languages that transformer architectures are capable of expressing.
Self-attention networks cannot process formal languages with hierarchical structure, such as A^n B^n, as noted by Hahn (2020) and Bhattamishra et al. (2020).
David Chalmers suspects that the residual non-structural properties of conscious experience will pose special problems for developing a formal language to describe them.
Writers transmit attitudes and emotions through devices such as sentence structure, word choice, punctuation, and the use of formal or informal language.

Word choice (diction) serves as an indicator of tone: formal language containing technical terms suggests a formal or scholarly tone, while casual language containing slang or contractions suggests an informal or conversational tone.

Tone is communicated through devices such as sentence structure, word choice, punctuation, and the use of formal or informal language, which collectively create a relationship between the writer and the audience.
Using overly formal language when communicating with someone who is using informal cues like emojis or casual punctuation can lead to a lack of rapport and communication breakdown.

J. Takahashi, T.D. Palmer, and Rusty Gage reported that retinoic acid and neurotrophins collaborate to regulate neurogenesis in adult-derived neural stem cell cultures, as published in the 1999 Journal of Neurobiology.

M. Takahashi, T.D. Palmer, J. Takahashi, and Rusty Gage observed widespread integration and survival of adult-derived neural progenitor cells in the developing optic retina, as published in the 1998 Molecular and Cellular Neurosciences.

During the 1950s, cults formed around individuals who claimed to have had contact with aliens, typically described as Venusians, Saturnians, or inhabitants of Clarion, a planet described as Earth's twin hidden by the moon.
Truman Bethurum, a contactee, stated that aliens from the planet Clarion feared humans would destroy Earth in a nuclear war, creating 'considerable confusion' among the inhabitants of outer space.

During the luteal phase, progesterone and estrogen levels rise and then fall if pregnancy does not occur, which contributes to premenstrual symptoms like mood changes, headaches, acne, bloating, and breast tenderness.
During the luteal phase, women may experience premenstrual symptoms (PMS) such as mood swings, fatigue, bloating, irritability, and breast tenderness due to fluctuating hormone levels.
During the luteal phase (Days 18-28), rising progesterone levels may cause premenstrual symptoms including bloating, irritability, fatigue, brain fog, and cravings.

The luteal phase is the second half of the menstrual cycle, occurring after ovulation, where the body prepares for a period if pregnancy has not occurred, often resulting in fatigue and PMS symptoms due to hormonal shifts.
Over 90% of women report experiencing some premenstrual symptoms, such as bloating, headaches, and moodiness.
Curcumin, resveratrol from red grapes, and piperine from black pepper reduce the synthesis of pro-inflammatory cytokines TNF-α, IL-1β, and IL-6 while preserving neuronal integrity.
Piperine, a bioactive compound found in Piper nigrum (black pepper), is effective against the MDR microorganisms Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus.
Black pepper (Piper guineense) acts as an ecofriendly antimicrobial agent against foodborne microorganisms.
The hymen is often perforated before the onset of menstruation to allow the flow of menses.
The uterine cycle consists of three phases: menses, proliferative, and secretory, which act to prepare the uterus for potential fertilization and pregnancy.
If no ovum is fertilized, the endometrial lining is shed, signifying the beginning of menses, the first phase of the uterine cycle.
Menstruation, also known as menses or a period, is the monthly shedding of the uterine lining, consisting of blood and tissue that flows from the uterus through the cervix and out of the vagina.
The follicular phase begins on the first day of menses and continues until ovulation, characterized by the development of ovarian follicles and lower temperatures on a basal body temperature chart.
The length of a menstrual cycle is measured as the number of days between the first day of menstrual bleeding of one cycle and the onset of menses of the next cycle.
Once menses begins, FSH levels decline due to the negative feedback of estrogen and the negative effects of inhibin B produced by the developing follicle.
The follicular phase begins on the first day of menses and continues until ovulation, characterized by the development of ovarian follicles.
If the corpus luteum is not rescued by pregnancy, it undergoes atresia, and the resulting progesterone withdrawal causes menses.
The first day of menses is identified as cycle day one, at which point estrogen levels are at a low point.
The pituitary gland secretes FSH and LH before the onset of menses, which stimulates the growth of several ovarian follicles.
Evolutionary psychology theory posits that humans are not adapted to work in large, anonymous bureaucratic structures with formal hierarchies.
Organizational barriers, such as rigid hierarchies, unclear job responsibilities, and excessive bureaucracy, cause communication delays and distortion.
Field et al. suggest that natural electro-communication stimuli function to avoid the deleterious effects of jamming signals, a role previously attributed solely to the jamming avoidance response.

Electric fish mitigate jamming signals by altering their electric organ discharge (EOD) characteristics to shift the frequency content of the jamming signal away from other electrosensory stimuli they need to detect, as established by Heiligenberg (1991).

When two electric fish are in close proximity, interference between their electric fields creates a jamming signal that hinders the animal's ability to electrolocate relevant stimuli like prey or object boundaries, as described by Ramcharitar et al. (2005).
A Real Estate Fund has a Beta of 1.1 and an expected return of 10%.
The Portfolio Allocation Game requires the allocation of 100% of capital among Stock A, Stock B, Bonds, and a Real Estate Fund to create a well-diversified portfolio that minimizes risk.
Jesper Kallestrup and Duncan Pritchard published the article 'Robust virtue epistemology and epistemic anti-individualism' in the Pacific Philosophical Quarterly in 2012.

Avram Hiller and R. Wolfe Randall published a paper titled 'Pluralism About Group Knowledge: A Reply to Jesper Kallestrup' in 2023, which responds to Jesper Kallestrup's 2022 critique of their earlier work on group knowledge.

Jesper Kallestrup (2022b) defines dispositional belief as: 'What matters for dispositional belief is that the proposition be preserved in memory from which it can effortlessly be recovered for active deployment in reasoning or planning.'
Jesper Kallestrup (2022a) characterizes dispositional belief as 'having information q readily available for endorsement.'
Jesper Kallestrup characterizes occurrent belief as 'a thought currently endorsed' in his 2022 paper.
Dormant dispositional beliefs do not require 'effortlessness' or 'ready availability' to count as beliefs, contrary to the requirements proposed by Jesper Kallestrup.

The paper 'Pluralism About Group Knowledge: A Reply to Jesper Kallestrup' is fully collaborative between the co-authors, and the alphabetical author ordering is not intended to denote primacy of authorship.

The publication of CycloneDX and SPDX v3 SBOM standards, alongside global government regulations, has led to an increase in the number of open source projects publishing Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs) with their components.
guacsec/trustify provides a searchable abstraction over CycloneDX and SPDX SBOMs, cross-referencing them against security advisories to identify vulnerabilities.

SoftwareDesignLab/SBOM-in-a-Box is a unified platform for SBOM generation using integrated open source tools, conversion between SPDX and CycloneDX formats, VEX generation, quality metrics, and comparison and merging.

The 'cyfinoid/aibommaker' project is a client-side web tool that analyzes GitHub repositories for AI/LLM usage and generates AI Bills of Materials (AIBOMs) in CycloneDX 1.7 and SPDX 3.0.1 formats, including detection of hardware, infrastructure, and governance components.

The 'Trusera/ai-bom' project is an AI Bill of Materials generator for agent workflows that scans n8n, LangGraph, and CrewAI workflows to identify AI components and generate SBOM output in CycloneDX and SPDX formats.
The OWASP CycloneDX project has launched an SBOM Exchange API.
SPDX and CycloneDX are the two primary standards for Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs).

CycloneDX is an SBOM standard originating from the Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP) community, designed for application security and supply chain component analysis, and has been extended to include software-as-a-service BOM (SaaSBOM).
The data formats used to generate and consume SBOMs include Software Package Data eXchange (SPDX), CycloneDX, and Software Identification (SWID) tags.
SPDX and CycloneDX are the two primary formats for a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM).
Treatment guidelines serve as essential references for standardized diagnosis and treatment protocols in clinical decision-making.

Paul Hager, Friederike Jungmann, Robbie Holland, Kunal Bhagat, Inga Hubrecht, Manuel Knauer, Jakob Vielhauer, Marcus Makowski, Rickmer Braren, Georgios Kaissis, et al. published 'Evaluation and mitigation of the limitations of large language models in clinical decision-making' in 2024.

The Medical Knowledge Graph (MKG) supports intricate clinical decision-making by accurately representing relationships between medications, such as beta-blockers like Labetalol and Propranolol, and treatments for cardiovascular care like Diltiazem and Nitroglycerin.

The 'trust' roadblock in HealthTech AI arises when systems fail to explain their outputs in a way that aligns with clinical decision-making, causing clinicians to hesitate in relying on the technology.
Hallucinations in AI applications used in medical devices may influence clinical decision-making and potentially jeopardize patient outcomes.

Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit systematic errors known as medical hallucinations, where the models generate incorrect or misleading medical information that can adversely affect clinical decision-making and patient outcomes.

Cognitive biases in medical practice, such as anchoring bias, confirmation bias, and availability bias, are systematic errors in judgment and reasoning that cause clinicians to deviate from optimal decision-making, often in high-stress or time-constrained environments.
Large Language Models primarily rely on statistical correlations learned from text rather than the causal reasoning required for effective clinical decision-making.

De Nicola et al. (2022) highlight the potential of knowledge graphs to enhance diagnostic accuracy by encoding complex medical relationships and facilitating structured reasoning in clinical decision making.

Clinical decision-making in healthcare faces three primary challenges: high data volume (including evidence and patient data), the prevalence of unstructured data (such as clinical notes, imaging reports, and discharge summaries), and non-deterministic, judgment-driven decision-making.

AgentClinic is a multimodal agent environment that treats clinical decision-making as a sequential task involving external tools and electronic health records, demonstrating that interactive diagnosis is more challenging for Large Language Models than static answering.
In the medical domain, factual accuracy, reliability, and interpretability are paramount for safe and effective clinical decision-making.

A substantial proportion of survey respondents reported encountering medical hallucinations—defined as factually incorrect yet plausible outputs with medical relevance—in critical tasks such as literature reviews and clinical decision-making.

To address communication voids caused by a lack of feedback, organizations can implement read receipts and acknowledgment systems, establish regular pulse surveys, create structured debriefing processes, use 360-degree feedback systems, and set up suggestion boxes or digital feedback portals.

To address the absence of feedback systems, organizations can implement read receipts and acknowledgment systems, establish regular pulse surveys, create structured debriefing processes, use 360-degree feedback systems, and set up suggestion boxes or digital feedback portals.

Fungi species from the genera Aspergillus, Fusarium, Verticillium, Trichoderma, Rhizopus, Penicillium, and Pleurotus can be used to synthesize nanoparticles of titanium oxide, copper, zinc, cadmium sulfide, gold, and silver, allowing for in situ incorporation of these materials into textiles according to Elsakhawy et al. (2022).
An expert described gold as being riskier than US stocks at the time of the report.

Examples of alternative investments include commodities (gold, oil, agricultural products), private equity, venture capital, hedge funds, private debt, infrastructure, and collectibles (art, wine, classic cars).

In bear markets, defined as periods of falling prices and pessimism, government bonds and cash typically outperform, defensive stock sectors such as utilities and consumer staples tend to decline less, alternative strategies like market-neutral hedge funds may provide diversification, and gold and certain commodities sometimes serve as safe havens.

Rising interest rates have a positive impact on cash (higher yields), a negative impact on bonds (declining prices), a mixed or negative impact on stocks (higher discount rates), a negative impact on real estate (higher financing costs), and an often negative impact on gold (higher opportunity cost).

Falling interest rates have a negative impact on cash (lower yields), a positive impact on bonds (increasing prices), a mixed or positive impact on stocks (lower discount rates), a positive impact on real estate (lower financing costs), and an often positive impact on gold (lower opportunity cost).

Interest rate changes impact asset classes in the following ways: Cash yields increase with rising rates and decrease with falling rates; Bond prices decline with rising rates and increase with falling rates; Stock valuations are mixed or negatively impacted by rising rates and mixed or positively impacted by falling rates; Real estate is negatively impacted by rising rates due to higher financing costs and positively impacted by falling rates; Gold is often negatively impacted by rising rates due to higher opportunity costs and often positively impacted by falling rates.

Alternative investments, including private equity, venture capital, hedge funds, commodities (gold, oil), and cryptocurrency, are classified as having a moderate to very high risk level and medium to very high return potential.
Gold is used for inflation protection, while cryptocurrencies carry extreme volatility.
Rising interest rates often have a negative impact on gold due to higher opportunity costs, while falling interest rates often have a positive impact on gold due to lower opportunity costs.
Gold is considered a safe-haven asset, serving as a store of value during periods of economic uncertainty.
Certain commodities, such as gold, increase in price during economic downturns.

An increase in the price of gold can signal that the economy is slowing and investors are seeking stability, while a decline in the price of gold signals that investors are moving capital into higher-risk assets.
Investors often shift capital to safe-haven assets, such as gold or government bonds, during periods of market instability caused by geopolitical events.
Nawshad Haque and Terry Norgate published 'The Greenhouse Gas Footprint of In-Situ Leaching of Uranium, Gold and Copper in Australia' in the Journal of Cleaner Production in December 2014.
Precious metals such as gold and silver serve as tools to hedge against inflation or store value.
Diversifying investments across asset classes, such as equity, debt, and gold, reduces risk and optimizes returns by stabilizing the portfolio against different market conditions.
Metallophiles are used in biomining to extract valuable metals like copper, gold, and rare earth elements from low-grade ores, reducing the need for toxic chemicals like cyanide.
Farm owners sought individuals in need of loans to secure permanent labor for the cultivation of cash crops like coffee, cocoa, and sugar and the exploitation of minerals like gold and tin.
Diversification reduces unsystematic risk by spreading investments across different asset classes, such as stocks, bonds, real estate, and gold.
While the long-term thesis for gold remains intact, BlackRock believes it is prudent to take gains from the asset following a significant rally.
The conduction system of the heart controls the regular pumping or heartbeat.
Heartbeat is synchronized with ventilation, as demonstrated in a 1998 study by Schäfer et al. published in Nature.
The heartbeat is controlled by the conduction system of the heart.
The regular pumping of the heart, or heartbeat, is controlled by the conduction system of the heart.
Bentov demonstrated that during meditation, the human aorta tunes to the beat of the heart, synchronizes with brain waves, and synchronizes with the Earth’s electrical field.

A randomized controlled trial (ChiCTR2000031418) published in Clinical Nutrition on May 22, 2024, investigated the effects of early nutritional intervention on oral mucositis and basic conditions in patients receiving radiotherapy for head and neck cancer.
In a study of head and neck cancer patients, grade 3 mucositis was observed in 25.0% of patients in the intervention group and 70.0% of patients in the nonintervention group.

Early nutritional intervention by a multidisciplinary nutritional support team improved body weight loss rates, mucositis severity, serum albumin levels, and hospital length of stay in head and neck cancer patients.

Beijing has historically suspected the United States of seeking to internally corrupt and transform China and the Communist world through 'peaceful evolution,' which China interprets as infiltration and subversion.
Beijing views the United States with mistrust, suspecting the U.S. of attempting to corrupt and transform China through 'peaceful evolution,' defined as internal infiltration and subversion.
Beijing suspects the United States of seeking to internally corrupt and transform China through 'peaceful evolution,' defined as infiltration and subversion from within.
The 1989 Tiananmen massacre and the subsequent collapse of the Soviet empire confirmed Chinese fears regarding American 'peaceful evolution.'
Welfare economics provides the theoretical underpinnings for government intervention, advocating for wealth redistribution to achieve a more equitable society.
Welfare economics, particularly the concepts of Pareto efficiency and social welfare functions, provides a framework for understanding the trade-offs between equity and efficiency.
Grunstein R. (2005b) discusses endocrine disorders in the context of sleep medicine in the 4th edition of 'Principles and Practice of Sleep Medicine', published by Elsevier/Saunders in Philadelphia.

Richard P. Allen, D. Picchietti, W.A. Hening, and C. Trenkwalder published 'Restless legs syndrome: Diagnostic criteria, special considerations, and epidemiology' in Sleep Medicine in 2003, based on a workshop at the National Institutes of Health.

Walsh, Dement, and Dinges authored a chapter titled 'Sleep medicine, public policy, and public health' in the 2005 book 'Principles and Practice of Sleep Medicine' (4th edition), which discusses the intersection of sleep medicine and public health policy.

To diagnose chronic inflammation, doctors review a patient's medical history and perform a physical exam, focusing on the pattern of painful joints, the presence of morning stiffness, and other symptoms.

The diagnosis of hormonal imbalances in men involves a medical history and symptom assessment, where a doctor asks detailed questions about symptoms, medical history, medications, and lifestyle factors, followed by a physical examination to identify signs such as changes in body composition, breast tissue enlargement, or testicular abnormalities.
Vankawala et al. published a 2021 meta-analysis in Frontiers in Psychiatry regarding hemodynamic responses to sub-anesthetic doses of ketamine in patients with psychiatric disorders.
Miller et al. (2019) investigated age and sex differences in sympathetic and hemodynamic responses to hypoxia and the cold pressor test.
Delgado, Nystrom, Fissell, Noll, and Fiez (2000) tracked hemodynamic responses to reward and punishment within the human striatum.
The researchers lagged time courses by 4 seconds to compensate for the delay in the hemodynamic response, following the SHOP paradigm.

Attacks on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines have been optimized to boost the ranking of malicious documents during the retrieval phase, leading to Vector and Embedding Weaknesses being classified as one of the OWASP top 10 use cases for LLM Security.

In 2025 and beyond, businesses must focus on identifying and blocking new and emerging threats while continuing to adhere to basic cybersecurity standards and best practices established by organizations like OWASP and NIST.
The OWASP CycloneDX project has launched an SBOM Exchange API.

CycloneDX is an SBOM standard originating from the Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP) community, designed for application security and supply chain component analysis, and has been extended to include software-as-a-service BOM (SaaSBOM).
The Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) identified 'Vulnerable and Outdated Components' as the 6th most critical risk to web applications in 2020, moving up from 9th place.

The Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) publishes a document titled 'Top 10 Ways to Attack LLMs' which outlines security vulnerabilities and attack vectors associated with Large Language Models.

A major telecommunications provider used a temporal knowledge graph to connect work orders and alerts, allowing the system to visually trace relationships and automatically identify the root cause of network incidents.
Excessive information from sources like emails, meetings, instant messages, and alerts makes it difficult for frontline workers to filter and identify important information.
Nofzinger et al. (2005) hypothesize that increased metabolism in emotional pathways in depressed patients may increase emotional arousal, which adversely affects sleep.
Increased metabolism in emotional pathways associated with depression may increase emotional arousal and adversely affect sleep.

Bradley, M. M., Miccoli, L., Escrig, M. A., & Lang, P. J. (2008) published 'The pupil as a measure of emotional arousal and autonomic activation' in the journal Psychophysiology, volume 45, pages 602–607.

Multimodal approaches in neuromarketing combine neurophysiological measures such as EEG, fMRI, eye-tracking, and GSR to capture cognitive processes like attention and reasoning, and affective dimensions like emotional arousal and valence.

The systematic review maps specific neural mechanisms, such as reward processing, attention, and emotional arousal, to individual consumer decision stages including problem recognition, information search, evaluation, choice, and post-purchase.
Pupillometry can monitor changes in pupil size in response to post-purchase experiences, reflecting emotional arousal and cognitive load, as noted by Bradley et al. (2008).
Pupil dilation is proportional to the strength of emotional arousal, allowing eye tracking to be used as a method for capturing emotional arousal levels.

While Galvanic Skin Response (GSR) is an effective measure for tracking emotional arousal, it cannot reveal emotional valence (the quality of the emotion), necessitating the use of complementary biometric sensors.

Computer-based facial coding can assess emotional valence (the quality of an emotional response) but is limited by its inability to assess emotional arousal (the intensity of an emotion), requiring the use of complementary biometric sensors.

The author of 'The Evolution of Transcendence' classifies transcendence states as states of high emotional and cognitive arousal, which manifest as ingroup-directed transcendence, theory of mind (ToM)-evoking transcendence, aesthetic transcendence, or epistemic transcendence.
Emotional arousal, product novelty, and social comparison exert a stronger influence on consumers than rational evaluation, creating opportunities for impulse purchases.
Y. Bai, M. Yao, and Y. Liu (2023) found that scarcity marketing affects consumer impulse buying in online shopping, with emotional arousal acting as a mediating role.
Whitaker JW published 'Feedlot empire: beef cattle feeding in Illinois and Iowa, 1840-1900' through The Iowa State University Press in 1975.

The Palisades plant in Michigan and Three Mile Island Unit 1 in Pennsylvania are two closed U.S. nuclear reactors proposed for restart, while the owner of the Duane Arnold plant in Iowa has filed an application for possible restart.

As of March 2013, the states of Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, District of Columbia, Hawaii, Illinois, Iowa, Kansas, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Montana, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Texas, Washington, and Wisconsin had renewable portfolio standards or goals.

R. Haug published an internal report for the Iowa Association of Municipal Utilities (IAMU) in 2003 regarding the Iowa Energy Project, which focused on wind and compressed air energy storage (CAES) development.

Jane Evans claimed to have lived six past lives: Lavonia (a tutor in 3rd century AD York), Rebecca (married to a Jewish money lender in 1189 York), Alison (an Egyptian woman and servant in 1450), Anna (a lady-in-waiting to Catherine of Aragon in the 16th century), Ann Tasker (a London seamstress in 1702), and Sister Grace (a Catholic nun in Iowa in the early 20th century).
Jane Evans claimed to have lived as Sister Grace, a Catholic nun in Iowa at the beginning of the twentieth century.
Colorado asserted that the court's opinion will not threaten the dual banking system and that Iowa’s opt-out of DIDMCA, which occurred over 40 years ago, has not caused significant problems.
Respiratory chain (RC) complexes facilitate processes within mitochondrial cristae.

While a glycolytic switch is necessary to acquire pluripotency, the early phases of induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) generation are characterized by an initial increase in oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) activity and the up-regulation of respiratory chain (RC) complexes.

Mitochondrial dysfunction increases with age, manifesting as the accumulation of mitochondrial DNA mutations, instability of respiratory chain complexes, proteostasis defects, reduced organelle renewal, and changes in mitochondrial dynamics.

Zingiber officinale (ginger) is the most cited medicinal species in the Quitexe ethnobotanical data, associated with the treatment of seven ailments: constipation, vomiting, indigestion, hypertension, influenza, nausea, and cough.

A study published in the 'Phytomedicine' journal reported that Echinacea extracts exhibit antiviral activity against viruses including influenza and herpes simplex virus by inhibiting viral entry into host cells and suppressing viral replication.
Standardized elderberry extracts are currently being evaluated for the prevention and treatment of influenza, building upon centuries of traditional use for respiratory ailments.
Research indicates that prolonged usage of Echinacea (coneflower), a widely utilized herbal treatment for colds and influenza, may diminish the efficacy of the immune system.
During viral infections such as SARS-CoV-2, RSV, Parvovirus B19, and Influenza, levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines are elevated and decrease upon viral clearance.
Influenza A and SARS-CoV-2 infections cause high viral replication in respiratory epithelial cells and mediate an aggressive inflammatory response that damages lung cells.
P. Yaqoob published 'Ageing, immunity and influenza: a role for probiotics?' in the Proceedings of the Nutrition Society in 2014.
Furman et al. (2015) found that cytomegalovirus infection enhances the immune response to influenza.
Mild infections, such as influenza, can exacerbate the condition of individuals who already have coronary plaques.
Despite changes in sleep duration during viral infections like rhinovirus or influenza, there are no reported differences in sleep quality or the number of awakenings compared to healthy individuals.

Statistical analysis in a study of global business communication shows a strong negative correlation between the severity of communication challenges and collaboration effectiveness (r = -0.54, p < 0.001).

Statistical analysis in the study 'Cross-Cultural Communication Challenges in Global Business' shows a strong negative correlation between the severity of communication challenges and collaboration effectiveness (r = -0.54, p < 0.001).
T cells, also known as T lymphocytes or CD3 cells, are a type of immune cell that can directly attack virus-infected cells or act as regulators of the immune system.

Natural Killer (NK) cells belong to the lymphoid lineage but do not express T cell (TCR/CD3 complex) or B cell (CD19) surface markers, and they are considered the third most important lymphocyte subset.

Natural Killer T (NKT) cells are a subset of natural killer cells defined by the co-expression of T cell markers (CD3/TCR complex) and characteristic surface receptors for NK cells (CD56, CD58, and CD161).

Peripheral mononuclear cells expressing CDKN2A are unable to survive cryopreservation, requiring the quantification of CDKN2A mRNA levels in peripheral T cells to be performed on freshly isolated CD3+ cells.

The Lai Ching-te administration in Taiwan has adopted increasingly provocative rhetoric toward Beijing while marginalizing the political opposition, which alarms Beijing and increases the risk of miscalculation among Washington, Beijing, and Taipei.

The Lai Ching-te administration in Taipei has adopted increasingly provocative rhetoric toward Beijing and has marginalized the political opposition, actions which alarm Beijing and increase the risk of miscalculation among Washington, Beijing, and Taipei.

Following the defeat of the Nationalist government in 1949, Chiang Kai-shek and thousands of his troops fled to Taiwan, where the United States supported his exiled Republic of China government in Taipei.
U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi visited Taipei in August 2022 to demonstrate U.S. support for Taiwan, despite months of warnings from Chinese officials against boosting ties with the island.

'Rabbit starvation' is a condition resulting from a diet based primarily on lean protein, characterized by starvation despite high-calorie intake, loss of body protein, protein toxicity, and death after several weeks (Stefansson 1944; Speth and Spielmann 1983).

In hunter-gatherer diets at latitudes above 40°, excessive consumption of lean meats from wild game can lead to a syndrome known as 'rabbit starvation' due to exceeding the maximum rate of urea synthesis (MRUS), as noted by Cordain et al. (2000).
The Inuit historically consumed the fattiest parts of animals to prevent 'rabbit starvation' and fed the leanest meats, such as muscle, to their dogs, as documented by Fediuk (2002).
For subsequent craniotomies in mice, the skin is reopened and small (~1 mm) craniotomies are created using a drill, with locations determined by Pinpoint [43] and stereotaxic coordinates.

The researchers will target brain areas in mice using stereotaxic coordinates and manipulator angles, verified by sensory response properties and/or reconstruction of probe trajectories using DiI or DiO coating with ex vivo brain imaging or post-hoc histology.
Syft is a command-line interface tool and library developed by Anchore for generating a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) from container images and filesystems.
Sbom-operator is a tool that catalogues all images of a Kubernetes cluster to multiple targets using Syft.
The 'davideshay/vulnscan' repository is a vulnerability scanner suite that utilizes Grype and Syft from Anchore.
Ckotzbauer/sbom-operator is a tool that catalogues all images of a Kubernetes cluster to multiple targets using Syft.
Susan Grieshaber is the Chair Professor and Head of the Department of Early Childhood Education at the Hong Kong Institute of Education.

Before joining the Hong Kong Institute of Education, Susan Grieshaber was a Professor of Early Years Education in the School of Early Childhood at Queensland University of Technology in Brisbane, Australia.
Susan Grieshaber co-edits the international journal 'Contemporary Issues in Early Childhood'.
Susan Grieshaber is conducting a sociological study on how the compulsory Australian learning framework for children aged 0–5 years influences educators' practice.
Susan Grieshaber is investigating the professional networks of early childhood educators in Hong Kong, Singapore, Taiwan, and Korea.

The 'Data Silo Problem' in enterprise AI occurs because standard document processing breaks enterprise knowledge into isolated chunks, causing the loss of critical semantic relationships that span different sections or documents.
Large Language Models are effective for general background knowledge, but they perform poorly when applied to specific enterprise knowledge.

To effectively ground LLM outputs in enterprise knowledge, a Knowledge Graph must contain knowledge from both database records and enterprise documents, a process Stardog calls 'extending AI safety by extending AI’s data reach.'

The issue of a plurality of worlds and their habitability gained force only when new speculative visions and scientific discoveries radically changed humanity's understanding of its place in the universe.

Alfred Russel Wallace defended an anthropocentric universe in his 1903 book, 'Man’s Place in the Universe: A Study of the Results of Scientific Research in Relation to the Unity or Plurality of Worlds', explicitly opposing the pluralist position.
S.J. Dick authored 'Plurality of Worlds: The Origin of the Extraterrestrial Life Debate from Democritus to Kant', published in Cambridge by Cambridge University Press in 1982.
S.J. Dick authored the chapter 'Plurality of Worlds' in the Encyclopedia of Cosmology, edited by N. Hetherington, published in New York by Garland in 1993, pages 502-512.
G. McColley and W. Miller authored the article 'St. Bonaventure, Francis Mayron, William Vorilong and the Doctrine of a Plurality of Worlds,' published in Speculum in 1937.
Michael Schmaus's theological textbook 'Katholische Dogmatik' (1957) echoes Joseph Pohle's conclusion regarding the plurality of inhabited worlds.
Italian priest-astronomers Angelo Secchi and Francesco Denza shared Joseph Pohle's position regarding the plurality of inhabited worlds.
D. Grasso authored the article 'La teologia e la pluralità dei mondi abitati' (Theology and the plurality of inhabited worlds), published in Civiltà Cattolica in 1952.

M.J. Crowe's book 'The Extraterrestrial Life Debate, 1750-1900: The Idea of a Plurality of Worlds from Kant to Lowell' (Cambridge University Press, 1986) provides a historical overview of concepts regarding extraterrestrial life from 1750 to 1900.

The historical concept of 'the plurality of worlds' referred to the existence of multiple, independent universes, each containing its own earth and celestial bodies, rather than multiple planets within a single universe.
Greek atomist philosophers concluded that a plurality of worlds exists.
Plato and Aristotle rejected the concept of a plurality of worlds.

Christian thinkers generally did not consider the possibility of extraterrestrial intelligent life on planets or moons until the thirteenth and fourteenth centuries, when pre-Christian notions regarding the plurality of worlds were questioned.

The concept of 'the plurality of worlds' in ancient Greek philosophy originally referred to multiple entire universes coexisting independently, each with its own earth and celestial bodies, rather than multiple planets within a single universe.
Thinkers within the atomist tradition of Greek philosophy concluded that a plurality of worlds exists.
Plato rejected the idea of a plurality of worlds but proposed that stars are living creatures, each possessing a soul.

Early Christian thinkers were aware of the pagan philosophical discussions regarding the plurality of worlds and continued the conversation by incorporating insights from the apostolic tradition of the Church.
Surviving documents from the Early Middle Ages show little speculation on the plurality of worlds, though the debate regarding the implications for extraterrestrial intelligent life continued.

Thomas Paine wrote in 'Age of Reason': "A belief in an infinite plurality of worlds, argued Paine, “renders the Christian system of faith at once little and ridiculous and scatters it in the mind like feathers in the air”. It isn’t possible to affirm both simultaneously, he wrote, and “he who thinks that he believes in both has thought but little of either.”"
Medieval Christian philosophers who objected to the "plurality of worlds" did so based on the cosmological model of pre-Christian Greek natural philosophy rather than Christian revelation.

The medieval thinkers William of Ockham, Jean Buridan, and Nicole Oresme concluded that there is no plurality of worlds in the sense of multiple entire universes, though their critiques identified weaknesses in the arguments of Aristotle and Thomas Aquinas.

By the end of the fifteenth century, the theological conversation regarding the plurality of worlds shifted focus from speculations about multiple entire universes to the possibility of multiple inhabited worlds within our own universe.
In 1863, Parisian preacher Père Joseph Félix announced to the Catholic faithful at Notre Dame Cathedral that the existence of a plurality of worlds was compatible with Christian beliefs.
Origen's notion of a plurality of worlds imagined them not as simultaneous, spatially separated worlds, but as multiple universes occurring in succession through time.
Grant McColley and H. W. Miller concluded that while William Vorilong is the earliest theologian noted to discuss the doctrine of a plurality of worlds, other theologians likely preceded him.

Surviving documents from the Early Middle Ages reveal very little speculation focused on the plurality of worlds, though philosophical and theological debates regarding the existence of extraterrestrial intelligent life continued.

Ancient and medieval Christian philosophers who objected to the 'plurality of worlds' did so based on the cosmological model of pre-Christian Greek natural philosophy rather than Christian revelation.
Scholastic thinkers concluded that there is no plurality of worlds, but their critiques helped identify weaknesses in the arguments of Aristotle and Aquinas regarding the nature of the universe.

Vorilong argued that the ancient philosopher Democritus would have understood the plurality of worlds correctly if he had viewed them as existing within the mind of God rather than as the result of random atomic interactions.

By the end of the fifteenth century, the intellectual focus regarding the plurality of worlds shifted from speculating about multiple entire universes to considering multiple worlds within our own universe, with an increased emphasis on the possibility of these worlds being inhabited by intelligent creatures.
Introducing a genuine scarcity element into a marketing campaign or conversation shifts consumer urgency, engagement, and decision speed.
R. Rettie, H. Robinson, and C. McIntosh (2021) evaluated the effectiveness of countdown timers in flash sale marketing as a tool to create consumer urgency.

The concept of sustainable business models is associated with related terms including product service systems (PSS), green business models, social business models, circular business models, and sharing business models.
Yunus, Moingeon, and Lehmann-Ortega (2010) published 'Building social business models: lessons from the grameen experience' in the journal Long Range Planning.

The combination of economic, environmental, and social business model patterns into a single business model is a necessary but not sufficient condition for achieving a truly sustainable business model.

Among 274 articles on sustainable business model patterns, "green", "ecological", and "environmental" business models appear in the titles of 13 articles, while "social business models" appear in the titles of 7 articles.
Kellogg Asset Management, LLC (KAM) provides investment management services to affiliates of Associated Banc-Corp (AB-C).

Associated Investment Services (AIS), Associated Bank, N.A. (ABNA), Associated Trust Company, N.A. (ATC), and Kellogg Asset Management, LLC (KAM) are direct or indirect, wholly-owned subsidiaries of Associated Banc-Corp (AB-C).
Associated Banc-Corp (AB-C) and its affiliates do not provide tax, legal, or accounting advice.
Psychiatrists should help arrange contact with an external debt advisor if a patient is experiencing significant creditor pressure and is not already receiving such support.

Some creditors contact individuals directly even when the individual has an external debt advisor, which can lead to conflicting repayment agreements where the creditor sets a higher repayment figure than the one agreed upon with the advisor.

Researchers have suggested that high levels of human disturbance from heavy vessel traffic servicing an industrial mine and increased killer whale predation are the primary causes for the shift in narwhal migratory pathways in the eastern Canadian Arctic.
Organisms must cope with unpredictable events, including social disruptions, predators, extreme weather events, and human disturbance.

Predator pressure cues indicate reduced access to exposed resources due to fear of capture; they are most reliable when predators are numerous or food resources are concentrated in unprotected areas, but increase error when perceived predators are not a danger (e.g., human disturbance).
The 'West-and-the-rest' dichotomization concept of culture characterizes cultures using dualities such as individualism/collectivism, egalitarianism/hierarchy, and rationality/irrationality.

The 'culture-nation-state equivalence' and 'West-and-the-rest' dichotomization views of culture lack scientific validity and differ from how the majority of anthropologists use the concept of culture.

Both the 'culture-nation-state equivalence' and 'West-and-the-rest' dichotomization models view cultures as constraints that force individuals toward specific behaviors, similar to how historical theories of racial instincts were used to explain individual conduct.
Commitment devices are a behavioral economics nudge approach that involves creating penalties for failing to follow through on a goal.
Automatic paycheck diversion into a separate savings account serves as a commitment device that motivates individuals to stick to savings goals by creating friction against reversing the setup.

The 'winner's curse' is a phenomenon where the winning bidder in an auction almost always overpays, meaning the winner loses money. This can be demonstrated by auctioning a jar of coins where the winning bid frequently exceeds the actual value of the coins inside.

The winner's curse was originally discovered in the field by engineers at an oil company who noticed that the rights they won to drill in specific spots yielded less oil than their geologists had predicted, because the auctions they won were not a random sample of their bids but rather their highest bids.
Max Bazerman and William Samuelson demonstrated the winner's curse phenomenon in laboratory settings after it had been identified in the field.
Sweetgrass (Hierochloë odorata) is a perennial grass belonging to the family Poaceae.

The most abundant genera among the 94 identified edible plant species are Moraceae and Rutaceae (8 species each), Poaceae (7), Fabaceae (6), Solanaceae (5), Brassicaceae and Rosaceae (4 species each), and Dioscoraceae, Zingiberaceae, and Meliaceae (3 species each).

The ethnobotanical study in Taishan County identified six plant families as the most commonly consumed wild edible plants: Asteraceae (13 species), Fabaceae (13 species), Lamiaceae (9 species), Acanthaceae (5 species), Rubiaceae (5 species), and Poaceae (5 species).
Asteraceae, Lamiaceae, Malvaceae, and Poaceae each had four recorded medicinal food plant species with a Relative Frequency of Citation (RFC) of 0.056.

In the ethnobotanical study conducted in Quitexe, Angola, the plant families Anacardiaceae, Asteraceae, and Malvaceae each contain four genera; Annonaceae, Arecaceae, Solanaceae, Lamiaceae, and Poaceae each contain three genera; and Burseraceae, Euphorbiaceae, Myrtaceae, and Zingiberaceae each contain two genera, with all other families represented by a single genus.

The 'Flora of Ethiopia and Eritrea' series, specifically volume 7 (Poaceae/Gramineae), was edited by I. Hedberg and S. Edwards and published in 1995 by the Department of Systematic Botany at Uppsala University and The National Herbarium at Addis Ababa University.

Mr. Bartlett argues that yield spread premiums and broker-lender relationships should be included in a 5 percent fee level, noting that the current legislative bill excludes back-end payments similar to the Home Ownership and Equity Protection Act (HOEPA).
The legislative bill discussed in the hearing prohibits prepayment penalties on all home loans, but restricts this prohibition to loans of $150,000 or smaller.

Mr. Bartlett argues that the prohibition on prepayment penalties should apply to loans up to the conforming loan level for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac (approximately $200,000) rather than the $150,000 limit included in the bill.
Longitudinal studies indicate that French workers have a marked preference for stable workstations over flex office arrangements.
According to the 'Mon bureau post-confinement' study by the ESSEC Business School Workplace Management Chair, desk sharing is better accepted by French office workers than flex office arrangements.
The material recovered near Roswell was consistent with a balloon device and most likely originated from one of the Project MOGUL balloons that had not been previously recovered.

Joe Nickell and co-editors conclude that the 1947 Roswell incident involved debris from Project Mogul, a classified U.S. military program that used high-altitude balloons to monitor Soviet nuclear activities.

Excessive sowing density generally decreases bast fiber content and quality, overall biomass yield, grain yield, panicle yield, stalk height and diameter, and partially, cellulose content, according to Burczyk et al. (2009).
For industrial hemp not intended for textile applications, sowing densities above 30 kg/ha can decrease stem height, stem diameter, grain yield, and biomass yield.
Phosphorus fertilization affects industrial hemp plant height, tensile strength, and elasticity of bast fibers, but does not affect grain, stem, or biomass yield.

Dual-purpose industrial hemp cultivars can benefit from nitrogen fertilization at rates of up to 200 kg/ha, which can increase biomass yields, grain yields, plant height, and stem diameter, according to Aubin et al. (2016) and Zheljazkov et al. (2023).
The use of red shade nets combined with the optimal ProLyks® dosage enhances both biomass yield and phytochemical production in the plant M. laevigata.

While maximum growth rate (μmax) is less commonly used to determine evolutionary fitness, biotechnology researchers often use μmax, survival rates in toxic chemical concentrations, and absolute biomass yield as appropriate fitness criteria.

In a 1997 study, Escherichia coli was evolved in chemostat cultures to select for improved growth on glycerol, resulting in phenotypic changes including altered colony morphology, increased growth rate, increased biomass yield, and decreased acetate formation.

In a study on Escherichia coli batch selection for growth on glycerol, rpoC RNA polymerase mutations were identified as a major source for improved growth, leading to a biomass yield increase of up to 40%, reduced acetate overflow, increased metabolic rate, and a lowered or total loss of motility.
Financial planners should advise clients to reduce their losses and let profits grow as part of a long-term investment strategy to engage in disciplined investment management.

An effective long-term investment strategy involves identifying a client's level of risk tolerance and risk perception, determining an appropriate asset allocation strategy, and rebalancing the client's portfolio on a yearly basis.
A semiconductor diode, also known as a p-n junction diode, is composed of p-type and n-type semiconductor materials.
In a p-n junction diode, the p-type semiconductor is doped with boron to create positive charge holes, while the n-type semiconductor is doped with antimony to add extra negative charge electrons.

When p-type and n-type semiconductor blocks are joined to form a p-n junction diode, extra electrons from the n-type combine with holes in the p-type to create a depletion zone that lacks free electrons or holes.

The documentary 'Close Encounters of the Fifth Kind: Contact Has Begun' asserts that forgotten spiritual knowledge holds the key to humans initiating contact with advanced extraterrestrial civilizations.

Dr. Steven Greer asserts in the documentary "Close Encounters of the Fifth Kind" that forgotten spiritual knowledge is the key to humans initiating contact with advanced extraterrestrial civilizations.
Thomson Reuters uses RAG to improve customer service by helping customer support executives quickly access relevant information from a curated database via a chatty interface.
Thomson Reuters' customer service solution uses embeddings to find relevant documents by splitting text into small chunks, embedding each chunk, and storing them in a vector database.

In Thomson Reuters' customer service solution, user questions are converted into vector embeddings and queried against a vector database to retrieve relevant documents, which are then used by a seq-to-seq model to generate a response.
Thomson Reuters' RAG solution improves accuracy and reduces hallucinations by tailoring answers using retrieved knowledge.
Sager CPA was the 2023 winner of the Thomson Reuters Luca Pacioli Advisory Accelerator Award.
Soto Zen master Dogen argued that insentient beings expound the teachings of the Buddha and wrote about the mind of fences, walls, tiles, and pebbles.
The 9th-century Shingon figure Kukai argued that natural objects such as rocks and stones are part of the supreme embodiment of the Buddha.
Tarynholz asserts that Jesus and Buddha taught very similar things and that Jesus taught Eastern philosophy, with much of it appearing in the Bible.
Eastern philosophy parallels Western psychology and modern science, and many of its concepts are present in the Bible, as taught by Jesus and Buddha.

Shoghi Effendi identified nine Earth-based Manifestations whose lives resulted in nine great world religions: the Bab, Bahá'u'lláh, the Sabean Founder, Krishna, Moses, Zoroaster, Buddha, Christ, and Muhammad.
Nigel Kerner posits that religious figures such as Christ, Buddha, and Mohammed came to affirm the dominance of non-physical reality and to point out the inferiority of physical reality.

In Buddhist mythology, the Buddha was tempted away from enlightenment by the demon Māra and his three daughters—Tanhā (Craving), Arati (Boredom), and Ragā (Lechery)—during his meditative retirement under the Bodhi tree.
Most scientists supported by Jeffrey Epstein held a non-political view of science as an engine of progress, according to Fahy.
Fahy observes that public debate around science in the United States, particularly regarding climate change and vaccination, has become sharper, more divisive, and intensely political.
Persistent allostatic states driven by chronic stressors are linked to cancer development.
The utility of the allostatic load index in predicting cancer development and prognosis is controversial, with findings varying across populations, cancer types, and assessment methods.
Dai, S. et al. (2020) asserted that chronic stress promotes cancer development, as published in Frontiers in Oncology.
Chronic alteration of the microenvironment that maintains multicellular organization remodels intracellular processes and induces ageing and cancer development.
Mitochondria play a role in cancer development, as discussed by Zong et al. in Molecular Cell in 2016.

Watanabe et al. (2017) proposed that nucleosome remodeling, DNA repair, and transcriptional regulation collectively establish negative feedback loops involved in cancer development and cellular aging.
Premature termination of reprogramming in vivo leads to cancer development through altered epigenetic regulation, according to Pennarossa et al. in Cell (2014).
Immunosenescence acts as a key player in cancer development, according to Lian et al. in a 2020 article in the Journal of Hematology & Oncology.
Disruptions to the epigenome are a hallmark of cancer development, making epigenetic functions an attractive target for cancer therapies.
Yang Y and Bedford MT (2013) reviewed the role of protein arginine methyltransferases in cancer development and progression.
Plasma ACTH, hGH, and cortisol levels follow specific patterns during the menstrual cycle, according to a 1975 study in the Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism.
Genazzani A.R. et al. published a study in 1975 in the Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism titled 'Pattern of plasma ACTH, hGH, and cortisol during menstrual cycle'.
A study led by Sanjay Shete, Ph.D., based on data from the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey, found that only 40% of American adults recognize consuming alcohol as a cancer risk.
Nearly 70% of American adults surveyed in the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey reported that they drink alcoholic beverages.
Just over 30% of American adults surveyed in the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey believe that cancer prevention is not possible.

family.name: The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US

facts.content: The authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' assert that solar energy has historically been viewed as a minor contributor to the US energy mix due to cost and intermittency constraints.

The authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' claim that cost competitiveness with US grid prices (6-10 US cents/kWh) can be attained by 2020 with the right incentives.

The authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' propose solving the intermittency problem of solar energy by integrating photovoltaics (PV) with compressed air energy storage (CAES) and extending thermal storage capability in concentrated solar power (CSP).

The authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' simulated CAES storage requirements using hourly load data for the entire US and 45-year solar irradiation data from the southwest region of the US under worst weather conditions.

Based on expected improvements in PV, CSP, and CAES technologies, the authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' project that solar energy has the potential to supply 69% of total US electricity needs and 35% of total US energy needs (electricity and fuel) by 2050.

The authors of the 2008 study 'The technical, geographical, and economic feasibility for solar energy to supply the energy needs of the US' project that by 2100, solar energy could supply over 90% of total US energy demand, and together with other renewables, 100% of total US energy demand, resulting in a 92% reduction in energy-related carbon dioxide emissions compared to 2005 levels.
Tyler Ellison has channeled spirit guides as well as plant and animal spirits.

The 'Life Between Lives' (LBL) spiritual geography, as described by Dr. Michael Newton's patients, consistently includes the presence of spirit guides, a Council of Elders, and a process for planning new incarnations.

The Quantum Healing Hypnosis Technique (QHHT) is claimed to provide benefits including the release of emotional blocks, the uncovering of root causes for physical or spiritual challenges, the understanding of one's soul purpose, and the receipt of messages from spiritual guides.

During a Quantum Healing Hypnosis (QHH) session, individuals may explore past lives, other dimensions, communicate with deceased loved ones, or receive guidance from spiritual beings such as angels or spirit guides.
The MAP system is a method used to connect with spirit guides for the purpose of emotional and physical healing.

During a Psilocybin-Assisted Quantum Hypnosis session, Kaya guided Sunhee to meet her spirit guides, where Sunhee viewed herself through the eyes of her guides and received positive, encouraging feedback about her essence.

A channeled message from a seeker's Spirit Guides expressed gratitude to Kaya for her role as an 'ovator' and 'keeper of the questions' during QHHT sessions, noting the importance of her service during a time of transition on the planet.

The author of the blog reports experiencing contact from spirit guides while performing routine household tasks or showering, noting that these entities appear to communicate when the brain is relaxed and open.
The author of the 'Quantum Explorations' blog reports that after practicing quantum healing, they developed the ability to communicate with spirit guides as easily as breathing.
Spiritual guides assist individuals with requests that align with their life plan, provided the assistance does not hinder necessary personal learning.
Spiritual guides communicate with individuals through signs such as songs, feelings, smells, books, and temporary relationships with people.
Humans are often conditioned from birth to ignore signs and communications from spiritual guides.
The number of spiritual guides an individual has is not fixed and can increase to a 'legion' during challenging life periods.

John Perry's theory of the essential indexical posits that genuine self-consciousness is linked to the ability to think and utter first-person thoughts.

John Perry describes an experience in a supermarket where he followed a trail of sugar, attempting to find the shopper with the torn sack to inform them of the mess, only to realize eventually that he was the shopper making the mess.

I once followed a trail of sugar on a supermarket floor, pushing my cart down the aisle on one side of a tall counter and back the aisle on the other, seeking the shopper with the torn sack to tell him he was making a mess. With each trip around the counter, the trail became thicker. But I seemed unable to catch up. Finally it dawned on me. I was the shopper I was trying to catch.

John Perry argues that first-personal content is 'self-locating' and therefore enables action, whereas non-first-personal content does not necessarily trigger the same action without additional self-identification.

John Perry distinguishes between the irreducibility of the first-personal way of thinking about oneself and the facts or states of affairs that make such thoughts true, noting that the latter are not irreducible.

John Perry argues that terms such as “I” are “essentially indexical” and pose a problem for the traditional Fregean view of belief, which treats belief as a two-place relation between a subject and a proposition.

John Perry argues that Fregean senses are descriptive, and no description is equivalent to an essential indexical, meaning no Fregean proposition can be the object of belief when one believes first-personally.
Cappelen and Dever (2013) argue against the philosophical claims surrounding the 'essential indexical', specifically challenging the arguments for it made by Perry and Lewis.

Cappelen and Dever argue that cases like Perry's shopper, often cited to show a special connection between self-consciousness and action, actually demonstrate that action explanation contexts are opaque and do not allow for substitution salva veritate.
John Perry (2001) proposes an account of 'I' that attributes to an utterance both singular content and 'reflexive content' (e.g., 'the speaker of this token is F').

John Perry (2012) proposes that certain sources of information are 'necessarily self-informative' or 'self-tracking', meaning a subject can only come to know of the instantiation of her own states through that specific form of experience.
John Perry's (1979) case of the 'messy shopper' highlights the connection between rational behavior and first-person thought in the context of the essential indexical.

Play supports the formation of safe, stable, and nurturing relationships between children and their caregivers, which is necessary for children to thrive.
Play supports cognitive growth, language and communication skills, social and emotional development, physical coordination, creativity, and overall school readiness in young children.

To support a child's development through play, parents should follow the child's lead by paying attention to their current activity, avoiding taking control or giving instructions, and asking open-ended questions to encourage deeper thinking and problem-solving.

Mutual joy, shared communication, and attunement (harmonious serve and return interactions) experienced during play between parents and children regulate the body's stress response.
Research indicates that play improves children's abilities to plan, organize, interact with others, and regulate emotions.

The PANDiet adequacy sub-score includes proteins, total carbohydrates, dietary fibre, total fats, 4 essential fatty acids, 11 vitamins, and 10 minerals.

The WISH, PHDI, and ELD-I indices, which use quantitative scoring, were positively correlated with the adequacy of most nutrients, including polyunsaturated fatty acids, vitamins (e.g., A, thiamine, B-6, and E), and minerals (e.g., manganese, magnesium, copper, and selenium).

The ELD-I dietary index showed significant differences of small magnitude for polyunsaturated fatty acids (ALA, DHA, and EPA+DHA), vitamins (A, riboflavin, niacin, pantothenic acid, B-6, D, and E), and minerals (iodine, magnesium, potassium, selenium, copper, and calcium).
The skeletal system utilizes minerals such as calcium and magnesium, which are absorbed during the digestive process, to facilitate bone growth and repair.

Deficiencies in select micronutrients, including vitamins and nutritionally essential minerals, can adversely affect innate and adaptive immunity, leading to increased vulnerability to infection and disease.
Consuming foods rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, such as omega-3-rich fish, berries, and green leafy vegetables, helps reduce chronic inflammation and supports tissue repair.
The immune system functions to allow beneficial substances like vitamins, minerals, proteins, and fatty acids to enter the body while turning away harmful substances like toxins and pathogens.
Raleigh OB/GYN recommends that women support their bodies during the follicular phase by eating foods rich in vitamins and minerals, specifically lean proteins, fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats.
Fresh fruits, vegetables, and whole grains are recommended sources of carbohydrates, fiber, vitamins, and minerals for maintaining health.

Europe's dependence on imports of new energy inputs, such as metals and minerals, represents a potential threat to the competitiveness of its new energy products, particularly amidst rising global trade tensions.

Approximately 80% of global sites for the production of energy fuels, minerals, and infrastructure are projected to experience a 10% increase in one-day maximum precipitation events in the near future.
Resource-rich developing countries often become heavily reliant on exporting natural resources like fossil fuels or minerals, leading to economies that are dependent on revenue from these exports.
Substances including minerals, vitamins, fatty acids, and antioxidant phytochemicals are potential candidates for postponing skeletal muscle aging and the onset of sarcopenia.
Philosophers Whitehead, Shan Gao, Michael Hoffman, and cognitive scientist Donald Hoffman have put forward panpsychist interpretations of quantum mechanics.
Shan Gao proposes a quantum theory of consciousness in his 2008 article, 'A quantum theory of consciousness'.
Frank D. from Killeen, TX, described Lori Lines as having a calming energy and being intuitive and easy to talk to following his QHHT session.
Frank D. reported feeling light while processing information received during his QHHT session with Lori Lines.

The Nix ecosystem faces challenges regarding bitwise reproducibility claims, which are examined in the context of software supply chain security.
Kingdom Plantae is classified into five subgroups: Thallophyta, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnosperms, and Angiosperms.
Gymnosperms are characterized by a well-differentiated plant body, the presence of vascular tissues, and the production of naked seeds that are not enclosed within a fruit.
Common examples of Gymnosperms include Cycas, Pinus, and Ephedra.

The plant kingdom is classified into two groups: Cryptogams, which are non-flowering and non-seed bearing plants (e.g., Thallophyta, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta), and Phanerogams, which are flowering and seed-bearing plants (e.g., Gymnosperms, Angiosperms).
Gymnosperms are nonflowering plants that possess unenclosed, naked seeds.
Gymnosperms (cone-bearing plants) first appeared in the Pennsylvanian period, approximately 319 million years ago (Ma).
Gymnosperms dominated the landscape during the early (Triassic) and middle (Jurassic) Mesozoic era.
Angiosperms (flowering plants) surpassed gymnosperms by the middle of the Cretaceous period, approximately 100 million years ago (Ma), in the late Mesozoic era.
Seed plants include gymnosperms (such as conifers) which produce "naked seeds," and angiosperms which produce flowers.
Seed plants include angiosperms and gymnosperms.
The evolutionary lineage of Charophytes, which eventually led to modern mosses, ferns, gymnosperms, and angiosperms, is considered monophyletic.
The Ginkgoales, a group of gymnosperms with only one surviving species, Ginkgo biloba, first appeared during the lower Jurassic period.
Gymnosperms expanded and reached their greatest diversity during the Mesozoic era (approximately 240 million years ago), supplanting ferns in the landscape.
The Jurassic period was characterized by the presence of cycads (palm-tree-like gymnosperms), Ginkgoales, and conifers.

Gymnosperms are a diverse group of seed plants that are paraphyletic and characterized by naked seeds, separate female and male gametes, wind pollination, and the presence of tracheids for water and solute transport.
Modern gymnosperms are classified into four taxa: Coniferophyta (conifers), Cycadophyta (cycads), Ginkgophyta (ginkgo), and Gnetophyta.
Gymnosperms dominate ecosystems such as the taiga (boreal forests) and alpine forests at high elevations due to their adaptation to cold temperatures and dry growth conditions.
Angiosperms formed a sister clade that developed in parallel with gymnosperms, rather than being derived from them.
The term 'Gymnosperm' is derived from the Greek words 'gymno' meaning naked and 'sperma' meaning seed.
Gnetophyta, a group consisting of 70 species across three genera, are considered the closest gymnosperm group to angiosperms because they produce true xylem tissue with vessel elements.
Ephedra foeminea, also known as the joint pine or Mormon tea, is a gymnosperm that relies on insects for pollination.
The Mediterranean plant species Ephedra foemina, also known as joint-pine or Mormon tea, is a gymnosperm that relies on insects for pollination.
I4CE has developed a 'Landscape of domestic climate finance' tool since 2013 to improve the knowledge base regarding investments and financial flows in sectors critical to the low-carbon transition.

The 'Landscape of Climate Finance' methodology is used by the Institute for Climate Economics (I4CE) to fuel public debate, assist decision-makers in assessing climate action performance, and identify remaining efforts.

facts.content: Lee et al. (2023) analyzed the benefits, limits, and risks of using GPT-4 as an AI chatbot for medical applications in the New England Journal of Medicine.

The researchers evaluated LLM hallucinations in the clinical domain using a structured annotation process based on the hallucination typology proposed by Hegselmann et al. (2024b) and the risk level framework from Asgari et al. (2024), utilizing New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) Case Reports for inferences.

The data collection process for the NEJM Medical Case Records Dataset involved two steps: (1) extracting URLs of all individual case record PDFs from the New England Journal of Medicine website by analyzing the website structure, and (2) using Selenium WebDriver with a Chrome browser to access each URL and download the files into a structured directory.

The research methodology described in the paper enabled the efficient, ethical, and high-quality extraction of New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) medical case records into a multi-modal dataset comprising text, images, and tables.

The dataset used to evaluate LLM hallucinations consists of 'Case Records of the Massachusetts General Hospital' published in The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM), as referenced in Brinkmann et al. (2024).

The dataset of NEJM case records used for evaluating LLM hallucinations includes clinical cases published between November 2000 and November 2018, specifically excluding cases related to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The researchers curated a dataset of 20 case reports from the New England Journal of Medicine, selected to highlight challenges such as complex differential diagnosis and detailed lab results.

The NEJM case reports used in the study required a substantial time commitment from annotators due to the reports' depth, clinical intricacy, and frequent presentation of unusual or diagnostically challenging conditions.
Annotators evaluating NEJM case reports were authorized and encouraged to use external medical resources, specifically UpToDate and PubMed, to inform their evaluations.
The researchers used case records from the Massachusetts General Hospital, published in The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM), to evaluate LLM hallucinations.
Gregorová's research in Polymer Degradation and Stability (2006) examines the stabilization effect of lignin when used in natural rubber.
Wood-derived bioplastics fabricated through delignification, redistribution, and vulcanization of natural rubber exhibit biodegradability, water stability, and high oxygen barrier properties.

Mellis (MIT Media Lab and Arduino), Limor Fried (Adafruit Industries), Phillip Torrone (Make and Adafruit Industries), Leah Buechley (MIT Media Lab), Chris Anderson (Wired and DIY Drones), Nathan Seidle (SparkFun Electronics), Alicia Gibb (Bug Labs), Massimo Banzi (Arduino), Tom Igoe (Arduino, ITP/NYU), Zach Smith (MakerBot Industries), Bre Pettis (MakerBot Industries), Andrew "bunnie" Huang (bunniestudios), Becky Stern (MAKE), Windell Oskay (Evil Mad Scientist Laboratories), John Wilbanks (Creative Commons), Jonathan Kuniholm (Open Prosthetics Project/Shared Design Alliance), Ayah Bdeir (littleBits.cc/Eyebeam/Creative Commons), David Ford (Blue Labs), Vitorino Ramos (LaSEEB - Evolutionary Systems and Biomedical Engineering Lab, IST, Technical University of Lisbon), Charles Gantt (The Makers Workbench), Dave Hrynkiw (Solarbotics Ltd. / HVW Technologies), Raúl C Oviedo (Ayuda Electronica Company), Stephen Eaton (Strobotics), Brent Picasso (Autosport Labs), Will Pickering (FunGizmos), Ronen Kadushin (Open Design), and Aaron Nielsen (.:oomlout:).
